markers.name: Magnitude-band base scrutiny weight (M1-M6)
markers.applicable_years: FY2016-2025 (FY2022 excluded; valid in all years incl. thin FY2019-2020 because mag_band derives only from amount, which is always populated).
markers.rationale: Validates and adopts the existing log-scale M1-M6 banding as the materiality backbone. The cutoffs (25K,100K,250K,1M,10M,100M) align cleanly with the >=100K population distribution: p50=228K lands in M5, p75=521K in M4, p90=1.71M in M3, p99=24.0M in M2 — i.e. each successive band captures a roughly order-of-magnitude rarer, higher-impact stratum. Within the >=100K headline pop (306,604 rows, $393.0B): M5 53.5%, M4 31.5%, M3 12.5%, M2 2.4%, M1 0.03%. This is a continuous base weight (NOT a binary alarm): it converts dollars into a bounded scrutiny multiplier so that every downstream marker is dollar-aware without a single $4.66B transfer drowning out 100 mid-size procurements. Recommended base weights per band (feeds the model as a lookup, not a filter): M6=0.5, M5=1.0, M4=1.4, M3=2.0, M2=3.0, M1=4.0. The bands are kept rather than re-cut because they are stable, interpretable, and already match the empirical percentile breaks.
markers.guardrails: (1) This is a BASE weight, never a standalone finding — a large amount alone is a lead only after the category_risk_multiplier and behavioral markers are applied; entitlement/scheduled flows must be down-weighted (see mag_x_category_severe). (2) Uses mag_band/amount only, so it degrades gracefully in thin years FY2019-2020 (no dependence on vid/appr_type/fp). (3) Do not treat M1/M2 as suspicious per se: 59-72% of dollars in AID/TRANSFERS/DEBT are legitimately >$10M scheduled disbursements. (4) The model should cap the materiality contribution so a single mega-transfer cannot dominate an agency's risk roll-up.

examples.why: M1 band; demonstrates why band alone is not a flag — this is a scheduled federal-grant TRANSFERS OUT, benign-by-construction, and the category multiplier must suppress it.
M2 band, PROFESSIONAL SERVICES (Hepatitis C Rx) — same dollar tier as a benign transfer but discretionary procurement, so higher net scrutiny once category multiplier applies.
M5 band is the modal stratum (53.5% of pop, base weight 1.0) — the calibration anchor against which higher bands are scaled.

markers.name: Category1 risk-weight map (expected-large vs discretionary)
markers.sql_predicate: category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','EMPLOYEE RELATED EXPENDITURES','FOOD','TRAVEL - IN-STATE','TRAVEL - OUT-OF-STATE') OR category1 IS NULL OR category1 IN ('N/A','CONVERSION')
markers.applicable_years: FY2016-2025 (FY2022 excluded). category1 is populated in all years including FY2019-2020, so the multiplier runs everywhere.
markers.rationale: Defines a per-category1 multiplier (range 0.3-1.5) applied on top of the magnitude base weight so the SAME dollar carries more scrutiny in discretionary procurement than in scheduled entitlement. Calibrated from observed risk profiles in the >=100K pop: AID and TRANSFERS show ~0% new-vendor and 59-61% of dollars >$10M (pure scheduled flow); DEBT SERVICE 71.5% >$10M and 0.7% manual rail (contractual). By contrast PROFESSIONAL SERVICES (17.9% new vendor), CAPITAL OUTLAY (22.4% new vendor), OTHER OPERATING (19.8% manual rail) are discretionary. PROPOSED MULTIPLIER MAP (assign in model as CASE over category1): AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS=0.4; TRANSFERS OUT=0.4; DEBT SERVICE=0.3; COST ALLOCATION AND INDIRECT COSTS=0.5; PERSONAL SERVICES=0.7; EMPLOYEE RELATED EXPENDITURES=0.9; FOOD=0.9; TRAVEL - IN-STATE=1.0; TRAVEL - OUT-OF-STATE=1.0; NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT=1.2; CAPITAL EQUIPMENT=1.2; CAPITAL OUTLAY=1.3; OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES=1.4; PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES=1.5; NULL=1.0; N/A=1.0; CONVERSION=0.5 (ledger artifact). The predicate above returns TRUE for the categories that receive a multiplier >=0.9 (the scrutiny-elevated set) so it is a runnable boolean; the full numeric map lives in notes.
markers.guardrails: (1) Down-weight (not exclude) entitlement categories: AID/TRANSFERS/DEBT keep a small non-zero multiplier (0.3-0.4) so a genuinely anomalous Medicaid payment can still surface via behavioral markers, but routine large disbursements are suppressed — this is what stops the $4.66B transfer and $318M Prop204 aid payment from topping the leaderboard. (2) NEVER do substring name matching anywhere in the category logic — category1 is a controlled vocabulary (17 distinct values verified), matched by exact equality only, eliminating the 'Aztec'->'ZTE' class of false positive. (3) NULL/N/A categories get a neutral 1.0 (not 0) so missing metadata does not hide an item nor manufacture a flag. (4) The multiplier is a weight, never a lead by itself; it only re-ranks the materiality contribution.

examples.why: AID / Prop204 direct payments — multiplier 0.4 correctly suppresses a benign scheduled Medicaid disbursement despite its M1 size.
PROFESSIONAL SERVICES inmate healthcare — multiplier 1.5 keeps a discretionary contracted-services payment in scope for review at the same dollar tier.
TRANSFERS OUT — 0% new vendor, scheduled; multiplier 0.4 demotes it relative to procurement of equal size.

markers.name: Severe materiality in discretionary procurement (>=$10M, discretionary category)
markers.sql_predicate: amount >= 10000000 AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT')
markers.applicable_years: FY2016-2025 (FY2022 excluded). Runs in thin years FY2019-2020 (uses only amount + category1).
markers.rationale: The composite headline marker of this family: it is the intersection of severe materiality (M1/M2, >=$10M) with discretionary procurement, where a single high-dollar item most warrants confirmation. Calibrated to fire on just 0.23% of the >=$100K pop (vs 2.42% for any >=$10M item) — explicitly carving out the 90% of >=$10M dollars that are benign scheduled AID/TRANSFERS/DEBT flows. This is the marker that surfaced the PSPRS round $99,000,000 unfunded-liability payments repeating across appropriations (round-dollar + apparent duplication) as a genuine lead, while leaving the $4.66B federal-grant transfer correctly unflagged. Severity 4 (not 5) because a large discretionary payment is review-worthy but not by itself anomalous — many are valid major contracts.
markers.guardrails: (1) Entitlement categories (AID/TRANSFERS/DEBT/COST ALLOCATION) are excluded by construction, so routine mega-disbursements never fire. (2) amount + category1 only -> safe in thin years, no false suppression from NULL vid/appr_type. (3) Exact category equality, no substring matching. (4) Produces a LEAD warranting review (confirm contract authority, deliverables, competitive procurement), never a finding; does not name a wrongdoer. (5) Pair with behavioral markers (round-dollar, dup_diffday, new-vendor, vendor_share) before escalating — many will be legitimate large contracts (e.g. NaphCare inmate healthcare).

examples.why: Round $99M OTHER OPERATING, repeated across appropriations same payee=agency — round-dollar + apparent duplication, confirm it is intended UAAL paydown not double-booking.
PROFESSIONAL SERVICES Hepatitis C Rx with payee masked as 'N/A' on a $33.9M discretionary item — confirm vendor identity and contract.

examples.agency: DEPT OF EMERGENCY AND MILITARY AFFAIRS
examples.why: $28.7M border-security fence professional services — discretionary, high-dollar, single vendor; warrants procurement/competition review.

markers.name: Material discretionary spend (>=$1M, discretionary category)
markers.sql_predicate: amount >= 1000000 AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','EMPLOYEE RELATED EXPENDITURES')
markers.applicable_years: FY2016-2025 (FY2022 excluded). Runs in thin years FY2019-2020 (amount + category1 only).
markers.rationale: The moderate-tier screening band: million-dollar-plus discretionary procurement, the natural population for routine spend review. Fires on 9,053 rows = 2.95% of the >=$100K pop, $38.0B exposure — a tight, well-bounded moderate marker (well under the 15% moderate ceiling) that scopes the discretionary-procurement universe for second-pass behavioral testing without dragging in the ~15% of all spend that is >=$1M (much of which is entitlement). Severity 3: a $1M discretionary item is material and review-eligible but common enough that it is a sorting band, not an alarm.
markers.guardrails: (1) Entitlement categories excluded so scheduled $1M+ Medicaid/transfer/debt rows do not flood the band. (2) amount + category1 only -> thin-year safe. (3) Exact category equality, no substring matching. (4) Intended as a SCOPE/sort band feeding behavioral markers, not a standalone lead — escalate only when combined with round-dollar, duplication, new-vendor, high vendor_share, manual rail, or robust_z signals. (5) Leads warranting review only; no accusations, no named wrongdoers.

examples.why: $30M contracted inmate healthcare — in-scope discretionary spend; legitimate but should pass behavioral screens.

examples.agency: DEPT OF EMERGENCY AND MILITARY AFFAIRS
examples.why: $28.7M border fence professional services — material discretionary procurement for second-pass review.
Round $99M OTHER OPERATING — in band and elevated by round-dollar/duplication co-markers.

specs.notes:
VALIDATION OF M1-M6 BANDS: Kept as-is. The log-scale cutoffs (25K/100K/250K/1M/10M/100M) match the empirical percentile structure of the >=100K population (p50=228K=M5, p75=521K=M4, p90=1.71M=M3, p99=24.0M=M2, p999=69.9M=M2/M1 boundary). Each band up is ~order-of-magnitude rarer and higher-impact, giving an interpretable, stable materiality ladder. No re-cut needed; I recommend treating mag_band as a continuous base weight (M6=0.5, M5=1.0, M4=1.4, M3=2.0, M2=3.0, M1=4.0) rather than a binary flag.

FULL category_risk_multiplier MAP (model implements as CASE over category1; range 0.3-1.5, all 17 distinct values + NULL covered):
  DEBT SERVICE 0.3 | AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS 0.4 | TRANSFERS OUT 0.4 | COST ALLOCATION AND INDIRECT COSTS 0.5 | CONVERSION 0.5 | PERSONAL SERVICES 0.7 | EMPLOYEE RELATED EXPENDITURES 0.9 | FOOD 0.9 | TRAVEL - IN-STATE 1.0 | TRAVEL - OUT-OF-STATE 1.0 | N/A 1.0 | NULL 1.0 | NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT 1.2 | CAPITAL EQUIPMENT 1.2 | CAPITAL OUTLAY 1.3 | OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES 1.4 | PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES 1.5.
Justification anchored in observed risk profile (>=100K pop): entitlement cats have ~0% new-vendor and 59-72% of dollars >$10M (scheduled), discretionary cats have 14-22% new-vendor and 14-25% manual rail. The sql_predicate fields for category_risk_multiplier and the mag markers are runnable booleans (verified against mart/hv_base.parquet); the numeric multiplier itself is a lookup, not a boolean, hence described here.

COMPOSITE SCORE this family feeds: materiality_weight = mag_base_weight(mag_band) * category_risk_multiplier(category1). This single product is the dollar-and-context-aware base that every behavioral marker family (round-dollar, duplication, new-vendor, vendor concentration, period-13, manual-rail, robust_z) multiplies into. It ensures a $30M discretionary contract outranks a $300M scheduled transfer.

OVERLAPS WITH OTHER FAMILIES: mag_x_category markers intentionally co-fire with round-dollar (PSPRS $99M), duplication (in_dup_diffday — PSPRS repeats), vendor-concentration (vendor_share>=50% on large single-vendor contracts), and masked-payee/NULL-vid markers ('N/A'/NULL payee on $33M Corrections Rx). These are not double-counts: this family supplies the materiality magnitude; the other families supply the anomaly signal. The model should multiply, then cap, so one mega-item cannot dominate an agency roll-up.

THIN-YEAR HANDLING: Every marker here uses only amount, mag_band, and category1 — all fully populated in FY2019-2020. None depend on vid, appr_type_canon, or fp_canon, so this family runs correctly in thin years and never penalizes a transaction for a system-wide-absent field. This is deliberate: materiality is the one family that must work everywhere as the backbone.

FEATURES NEEDED BUT ABSENT: none for this family — mag_band and category1 are present and sufficient. (Behavioral families that multiply into this score do reference dup_*, vendor_*, robust_z, payment_method, which exist.) No placeholder columns required.

FRAMING: All four markers produce leads warranting review, not findings of fraud; none names a wrongdoer. Name/category matching is exact-equality over a 17-value controlled vocabulary, so the 'Aztec'->'ZTE' substring false-positive class cannot occur in this family.

markers.name: Exact $1M-multiple in discretionary procurement (arms-length)
markers.sql_predicate: amount >= 100000 AND is_round_1m AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','EMPLOYEE RELATED EXPENDITURES','FOOD','TRAVEL - IN-STATE','TRAVEL - OUT-OF-STATE') AND (payee IS NULL OR (agency <> payee AND NOT regexp_matches(upper(payee), '\b' || regexp_escape(upper(agency)) || '\b') AND NOT regexp_matches(upper(agency), '\b' || regexp_escape(upper(payee)) || '\b'))) AND upper(coalesce(payee,'X')) NOT IN ('N/A','NULL','NONE','VARIOUS') AND (payment_method IS NULL OR payment_method NOT IN ('INTERNAL','JOURNAL VOUCHER','COST ALLOCATION'))
markers.applicable_years: FY2016-2025 (FY2022 not in mart). Runs in all years incl. thin FY2019/2020 because it depends only on amount, category1, payee, agency, payment_method — none of the fields that are NULL system-wide in FY19/20 (vid, appr_type_canon, fp_canon).
markers.rationale: A payment that lands on an EXACT multiple of $1,000,000 is overwhelmingly likely to be an estimated/negotiated/capped/manually-entered figure rather than an invoice-derived total (which would carry odd dollars and cents). Restricting to discretionary-procurement category1 values (the highest auditor scrutiny weight per the risk framework) concentrates the signal on contracts/services/capital where a clean million is genuinely anomalous: in PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES 74% of >=$100K payments carry cents, in OTHER OPERATING 67% do. Entitlement/scheduled categories (AID TO ORGS, TRANSFERS OUT, DEBT SERVICE) — which are legitimately round (TRANSFERS OUT is 12.5% round-100k / 6% round-1m by design) — are excluded so the marker never fires on benign appropriated allotments. Calibrated to 0.036% of the >=$100K population, this is a strong standalone scrutiny signal worth a weight of 5.
markers.guardrails: (1) Category whitelist EXCLUDES entitlement/scheduled families (AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS, TRANSFERS OUT, DEBT SERVICE, COST ALLOCATION AND INDIRECT COSTS, PERSONAL SERVICES) whose round amounts are benign-by-construction. (2) Arms-length filter: agency<>payee AND word-BOUNDARY (regexp_matches with \b, never blind substring — the ZTE/Aztec lesson) name-containment in either direction, to suppress same-entity book transfers that evade exact-equality (e.g. UNIVERSITY OF ARIZONA -> 'THE UNIVERSITY OF ARIZONA'/'...(FINANCIAL SERVICES OFFICE)', PSPRS self-pay). Name overlap is used only to SUPPRESS (never to flag), so substring risk is one-directional/conservative. (3) Excludes INTERNAL/JOURNAL VOUCHER/COST ALLOCATION rails (non-arms-length fund movements). (4) Junk/placeholder payees ('N/A' etc.) excluded. (5) amount>=100000 floor. No dependence on thin-year-NULL fields, so it degrades gracefully and never penalizes a row for a system-wide absent field. These guardrails removed ~48% of raw round_1m discretionary hits (213 -> 110) that were internal/self transfers.

examples.why: Exact $90,000,000 to an external bank coded OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES — a perfectly round nine-figure amount detached from any invoice; lead to confirm the underlying obligation and pricing basis.
Exact $23,000,000 to ASU under PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES; clean-million services payment warrants review of the contract/scope-of-work supporting the round figure.
Exact $80,000,000 pension contribution coded EMPLOYEE RELATED; though partly scheduled, the exact-million roundness is an item to confirm against the actuarial schedule.

markers.name: Exact $100K-multiple (sub-$1M) in discretionary procurement (arms-length)
markers.sql_predicate: amount >= 100000 AND is_round_100k AND NOT is_round_1m AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','EMPLOYEE RELATED EXPENDITURES','FOOD','TRAVEL - IN-STATE','TRAVEL - OUT-OF-STATE') AND (payee IS NULL OR (agency <> payee AND NOT regexp_matches(upper(payee), '\b' || regexp_escape(upper(agency)) || '\b') AND NOT regexp_matches(upper(agency), '\b' || regexp_escape(upper(payee)) || '\b'))) AND upper(coalesce(payee,'X')) NOT IN ('N/A','NULL','NONE','VARIOUS') AND (payment_method IS NULL OR payment_method NOT IN ('INTERNAL','JOURNAL VOUCHER','COST ALLOCATION'))
markers.applicable_years: FY2016-2025 (FY2022 not in mart). Runs in all years incl. thin FY2019/2020 (depends only on amount, category1, payee, agency, payment_method).
markers.rationale: The next coarser roundness tier: an EXACT multiple of $100,000 that is NOT also a $1M multiple (that band is captured by the severity-5 marker, so the two are disjoint by construction — verified 0 overlap). A clean $100K step in discretionary procurement, where invoice-derived totals normally carry cents, is the signature of an estimated/negotiated/manual amount. Slightly weaker than the $1M-exact tier because $100K steps are more common in legitimately-rounded purchase orders, hence weight 4. Calibrated to 0.24% of the >=$100K population — comfortably in the 'severe/high' band (<5%).
markers.guardrails: Identical guardrail stack to the $1M marker: discretionary-only category whitelist (excludes entitlement/scheduled families that are legitimately round), word-boundary same-entity suppression (regexp \b, never blind substring), INTERNAL/JV/COST-ALLOCATION rail exclusion, junk-payee exclusion, amount>=100000 floor, no thin-year-NULL field dependence. NOT is_round_1m enforces clean tiering vs the severity-5 marker (no double-flagging the same row).

examples.why: Exact $12,500,000 ($100K-multiple, not $1M) to an external LLC under OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES — round to the hundred-thousand, suggesting a negotiated/capped amount; item to confirm against deliverables.
Exact $8,700,000 pension-related payment; clean $100K step warrants reconciliation to the contribution schedule.

examples.agency: DEPT OF EMERGENCY AND MILITARY AFFAIRS
examples.why: Round $100K-multiple to a disaster-response contractor under PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES; lead to confirm whether the round figure is a milestone draw vs an actual-cost invoice.

markers.name: Exact whole-dollar (no cents, not even $10K-round) in cents-normal procurement
markers.sql_predicate: amount >= 100000 AND is_whole_dollar AND NOT is_round_10k AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','EMPLOYEE RELATED EXPENDITURES','FOOD') AND (payee IS NULL OR (agency <> payee AND NOT regexp_matches(upper(payee), '\b' || regexp_escape(upper(agency)) || '\b') AND NOT regexp_matches(upper(agency), '\b' || regexp_escape(upper(payee)) || '\b'))) AND upper(coalesce(payee,'X')) NOT IN ('N/A','NULL','NONE','VARIOUS') AND (payment_method IS NULL OR payment_method NOT IN ('INTERNAL','JOURNAL VOUCHER','COST ALLOCATION'))
markers.applicable_years: FY2016-2025 (FY2022 not in mart). Even FY2016-2018 (object_no/invoice_number-rich) need no special field; runs uniformly incl. thin FY2019/2020.
markers.rationale: The 'too-clean amount in a category that normally has cents' signal. Restricted to the six discretionary-procurement category1 values where carrying cents is the documented norm (PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES 74% carry cents, OTHER OPERATING 67%, CAPITAL OUTLAY 89%). A payment with exactly zero cents AND not even a $10K-round figure (so it is an 'odd whole number' like $28,709,669 or $28,323,000 rather than an obviously-round $X00,000) is statistically detached from invoice arithmetic and points to a negotiated/capped/manually-keyed contract amount. Deliberately the broadest tier (2.65% of the >=$100K pop, squarely in the moderate <15% band) and disjoint from the two round markers above (NOT is_round_10k excludes everything they capture; verified 0 overlap), so weight 3 — a supporting signal best combined with vendor/duplicate markers.
markers.guardrails: (1) Category whitelist restricted to the SIX categories empirically shown to carry cents by default — DEBT SERVICE (44% cents) and TRANSFERS OUT (47% cents) and PERSONAL SERVICES/payroll-driven categories are excluded precisely because whole-dollar amounts there are normal, not anomalous. (2) NOT is_round_10k removes the obviously/legitimately-round purchase-order amounts (those are the domain of the round-100k/1m markers) so this tier isolates the 'oddly exact whole number' band. (3) Same word-boundary arms-length same-entity suppression (this guardrail alone removed 719 intra-University-of-Arizona self-transfers + 457 'N/A' placeholder payees that otherwise dominated the raw output). (4) INTERNAL/JV/COST-ALLOCATION rail exclusion. (5) amount>=100000 floor; no thin-year-NULL dependence. Confirmed not concentrated in a single benign payer after guardrails; FY distribution is flat ~720-1090/yr across 2016-2025.

examples.agency: DEPT OF EMERGENCY AND MILITARY AFFAIRS
examples.why: Exact $28,709,669.00 (whole dollars, no cents, not $10K-round) to a disaster-response contractor under PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES — large arms-length services payment with suspiciously cents-free total; item to confirm against invoiced costs.
Exact $28,323,000.00 to a prison-healthcare vendor; whole-dollar in a cents-normal services category points to a negotiated capitation/contract figure to reconcile to actuals.

Exact $12,000,000.00 CAPITAL OUTLAY to a law firm/PC — clean whole-dollar capital payment warranting review of the settlement/acquisition basis. (Note: distinct external payee, passes word-boundary arms-length check.)

specs.notes:
CALIBRATION: All headline stats restricted to amount>=100000 (pop = 306,604). The three markers form a clean, mutually-exclusive roundness tier with verified 0 row overlap: A = exact $1M-multiple; B = exact $100K-multiple but NOT $1M (NOT is_round_1m); C = whole-dollar but NOT even $10K-round (NOT is_round_10k, which excludes everything A and B capture). They can be summed without double-counting. Combined coverage ~2.92% of the >=$100K pop ($5.36B exposure).

BENIGN-BY-CONSTRUCTION ARTIFACTS FOUND AND SUPPRESSED (the core calibration work): (1) Entitlement/scheduled categories are legitimately round — TRANSFERS OUT is 12.5% round-100k and 6% round-1m, DEBT SERVICE 55% whole-dollar — all excluded via category whitelist. (2) Same-entity internal transfers dominated the raw top hits: PSPRS paying itself ($99M unfunded-liability entries coded OTHER OPERATING), and UNIVERSITY OF ARIZONA -> 'THE UNIVERSITY OF ARIZONA' / '...(FINANCIAL SERVICES OFFICE)' where the payee string differs from the agency so exact-equality misses it. Suppressed via WORD-BOUNDARY (regexp \\b) name-containment in both directions plus INTERNAL/JV/COST-ALLOCATION rail exclusion. Raw round_1m discretionary dropped 213->110 (48% were book entries). NOTE on the ZTE/Aztec lesson: word-boundary name matching here is used ONLY to SUPPRESS (exclude) rows, never to FLAG them, so even a false name-overlap can only cause a conservative under-flag, never a false accusation — the dangerous direction is eliminated.

GRACEFUL DEGRADATION: None of the three predicates reference vid, appr_type_canon, fp_canon, object_no, or invoice_number (the fields NULL in thin years or sparsely populated). They depend only on amount, the round-flag columns, category1, agency, payee, payment_method — all present FY2016-2025. So they run identically in thin FY2019/2020 and never penalize a row for a system-wide-absent field. FY distribution is flat, confirming no thin-year distortion.

OVERLAPS WITH OTHER FAMILIES: (a) round amounts correlate with vendor_first_appearance and dup markers — recommend treating these as INDEPENDENT additive signals (a round amount to a brand-new vendor, or a round amount in a duplicate-signature cluster, is materially more suspicious than either alone). (b) The manual-rail exclusion here means a separate 'manual rail' marker family should own JV/INTERNAL roundness if desired; I deliberately ceded those to avoid double-counting non-arms-length book entries as procurement roundness. (c) robust_z_in_agencycat (peer-deviation) overlaps: a round amount that is ALSO a peer outlier (robust_z>=10) is a stronger lead — compose rather than merge.

FEATURES THAT WOULD STRENGTHEN THESE MARKERS BUT ARE ABSENT FROM THE MART: (1) a boolean 'same_legal_entity(agency,payee)' resolved via canonical vendor identity (vid-based) rather than name regex — would let me drop the regex suppression heuristic and capture self-transfers that share no name tokens; I approximate it with the word-boundary name check. (2) contract_number/contract_name are only ~2-6% populated, so I could not require 'round amount WITHOUT a supporting contract' which would sharpen marker C; if contract linkage were dense, 'is_whole_dollar AND contract_number IS NULL' would be a high-value refinement. (3) a per-payee historical cents-frequency would let me flag vendors who normally invoice with cents but suddenly bill a round number — not derivable from the current mart at row level.

markers.name: Different-day exact-duplicate payment to a procurement vendor
markers.sql_predicate: amount >= 100000 AND in_dup_diffday AND category1 NOT IN ('AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS','TRANSFERS OUT','DEBT SERVICE')
markers.applicable_years: FY2017-2018, FY2021, FY2023-2025. Fires 0 times in FY2016/FY2019/FY2020 because the underlying duplicate signature relies on transaction_reference_id / invoice_number, which are absent in those years - so the marker degrades gracefully to zero rather than mis-firing in thin years. (FY2022 already excluded from mart.)
markers.rationale: in_dup_diffday marks rows belonging to a duplicate signature cluster (same agency + payee/vid + exact amount + transaction_reference_id) whose members post on DIFFERENT days (dup_day_span >= 1). For discretionary procurement (services, equipment, capital), the same vendor receiving the identical dollar amount under the identical PO/reference on separate days is the single strongest standalone duplicate-disbursement lead - it cannot be explained as an intra-batch accounting split. Restricting to non-entitlement categories removes the Medicaid/transfers/debt-service scheduled-batch noise that dominates the raw flag (526 of 533 raw diffday rows are AID-TO-ORGANIZATIONS). Weighting different-day repeats far above same-day batches per the family brief.
markers.guardrails: Excludes entitlement/scheduled categories (AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS = Medicaid capitation; TRANSFERS OUT; DEBT SERVICE) where same-reference repeats are legitimate. No name/substring matching is used (signature is structural: id+amount+reference), so the Aztec/ZTE false-positive class cannot occur. Lead only - identical amounts paid weeks apart may be contractually-scheduled installments under one PO; confirm against the contract/invoice before drawing any conclusion. Names no wrongdoer.

examples.why: Identical $112,560 paid THREE times (2023-06-27, 09-19, 11-09) under the same reference GAXIV1HLAINV001221781; confirm whether one obligation was disbursed three times.
Identical $500,000 paid twice 31 days apart (2023-12-19, 2024-01-19) under the same PO reference GAXLDAPO-3923.
Identical $145,694 paid twice 150 days apart (2022-07-20, 2022-12-17) under the same reference GAXIV1PSAINV000963404.

markers.name: Same-day exact-duplicate payment to an external procurement vendor
markers.sql_predicate: amount >= 100000 AND in_dup_signature AND NOT in_dup_diffday AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT') AND payee <> agency
markers.applicable_years: FY2017-2018, FY2021, FY2023-2025 (same signature-availability constraint as dup_diffday_discretionary; zero in FY2016/2019/2020).
markers.rationale: in_dup_signature & NOT in_dup_diffday isolates same-day clusters (dup_day_span = 0) of two or more rows sharing the identical agency+payee+amount+reference signature. Restricted to discretionary procurement categories and to external vendors (payee <> agency, which removes intra-government self-pay such as the PSPRS $99M x10 pension-funding batch and similar legitimate same-reference splits). A real outside vendor receiving the exact same large amount twice the same day under one reference is a moderate duplicate-payment lead - more often a legitimate two-line split of one invoice than a true double-pay, hence severity 3 rather than 5.
markers.guardrails: payee <> agency suppresses the pension-paydown / intra-fund self-payment batches the brief calls out as benign (PSPRS, fund-to-fund). Entitlement categories excluded. Structural signature match only - no name/substring logic, so no Aztec/ZTE-class risk. Same-day exact repeats are frequently legitimate invoice splits; this is an item to confirm against AP detail, never an accusation.

examples.why: Two identical $1,107,750 ACH payments on 2024-09-10 under the same reference GAXHCAADM250000390; confirm not a double-disbursement.
Two identical $1,003,374 warrants on 2018-06-26 under the same reference PRCPZ1CHAPR0000859606.
Two identical round $1,000,000 ACH payments on 2022-09-29 under the same reference GAXIV1EDAINV001003030.

markers.name: Different-day exact-duplicate payment (all categories, broad sweep)
markers.sql_predicate: amount >= 100000 AND in_dup_diffday
markers.applicable_years: FY2017-2018, FY2021, FY2023-2025 (zero in FY2016/2019/2020 by graceful degradation).
markers.rationale: Broad, low-severity companion to dup_diffday_discretionary that retains the entitlement rows for completeness/coverage. Captures every different-day exact-amount repeat under one reference, the vast majority being AHCCCS Medicaid managed-care/provider amounts (e.g., cross-fiscal-year reprocessing of the same capitation/recoupment line). These are still legitimately items to confirm (a reissue vs. an unintended second disbursement), but for entitlement spend identical reference repeats are usually scheduled/expected, so suspicion-per-txn is low.
markers.guardrails: Severity deliberately low because the population is dominated by entitlement spend where same-reference batches are legitimate (Medicaid capitation, provider recoupments, DES transfers). Use only as a coverage net, not a standalone red flag; route entitlement hits to a lighter reconciliation queue. Structural match only - no name matching. Names no wrongdoer.

examples.why: Identical $63,279,037 posted 25 days apart (2024-10-07, 2024-11-01) under reference GAXNEHCAPMMS25045031; likely a legitimate Medicaid reprocessing but confirm it is not a duplicated transfer.
Identical $39,559,908 capitation on consecutive days (2024-08-29, 08-30) under reference GAXCMHCAPRG250000018.
Identical $189,968 posted 428 days apart across FY2024/FY2025 under the same reference GAXCMHCAPRG240000111 - cross-year reprocessing to confirm.

markers.name: Possible split disbursement: clustered sub-threshold payments aggregating over a control limit (REQUIRES NEW FEATURE)
markers.sql_predicate: amount >= 100000 AND vid IS NOT NULL AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT') AND split_cluster_flag = TRUE
markers.applicable_years: FY2016-2018, FY2021, FY2023-2025 (requires vid, which is NULL system-wide in FY2019/2020, so the marker cannot run there - state honestly).
markers.rationale: The split angle requested by the brief: several distinct payments to the same vid+agency in a short window, each individually below a delegated-authority / competitive-bid threshold, that aggregate above that threshold - a pattern consistent with splitting one obligation to stay under an approval limit. This CANNOT be expressed soundly on the current mart: prototyping a naive version (same vid+agency+day, >=5 distinct txns < $250K summing >= $1M) returned 160 clusters / 2,302 member txns that are overwhelmingly legitimate high-frequency vendors (Cardinal Health pharma to Corrections, Axon, Accenture, SHI IT reseller, ADOT construction firms, a 40-vehicle PFVT fleet buy). Distinguishing a true threshold-evasion split from normal high-volume AP requires features absent from the mart.
markers.guardrails: Restricted to discretionary categories, real vendors (vid IS NOT NULL, which also prevents firing in thin FY19/20 years). MUST exclude high-frequency-by-design vendors before flagging, or it reproduces the Aztec/ZTE-class false-positive problem on benign volume. Lead only.

examples.why: Illustrative of the FALSE-POSITIVE risk: 25 same-day sub-$250K invoices summing $3.4M - benign pharma distribution volume, NOT a split. Shown to motivate why the naive predicate is suppressed.
19 same-day sub-$250K lines summing $3.1M - routine equipment buy, illustrating the benign pattern the real marker must exclude.

specs.notes:
CALIBRATION/PREVALENCE (all restricted to amount>=100000; >=100K pop = 306,604):
 - dup_diffday_discretionary: 7 txns (~0.002%), $1.63M. Extremely rare, very high precision -> severity 5.
 - dup_sameday_discretionary_external: 494 txns (0.16%), $114.7M -> severity 3 (moderate, <5% severe band easily met).
 - dup_diffday_all_incl_entitlement: 533 txns (0.17%), $1.357B; entitlement-dominated -> severity 2 coverage net.
 - split_under_threshold_window: NOT computable on current mart; returned as placeholder with severity 2 and count/exposure 0.

DUP SIGNATURE DEFINITION (reverse-engineered from data): in_dup_signature groups rows sharing agency + payee/vid + EXACT amount + transaction_reference_id; in_dup_diffday is the subset whose members span >=1 calendar day (dup_day_span>=1). Because the signature depends on transaction_reference_id/invoice_number, the flags fire 0 times in FY2016, FY2019, FY2020 - this is the desired graceful degradation, not a bug; vendor/period/appr-type NULLs in FY19/20 are therefore never penalized.

OVERLAPS:
 - dup_diffday_discretionary is a strict subset of dup_diffday_all_incl_entitlement (the 7 vs 533). If both run, dedupe by taking the highest-severity hit per row; do not double-count exposure.
 - dup_sameday_discretionary_external and the diffday markers are mutually exclusive by construction (NOT in_dup_diffday vs in_dup_diffday).
 - All three structural markers overlap with any round-number marker (e.g. NCS Pearson $1,000,000) and with manual-rail markers; treat as corroborating, not additive.

MISSING FEATURES NEEDED TO MAKE THE SPLIT MARKER REAL (precise spec for the mart owner):
 1) split_cluster_flag (boolean): TRUE iff, within a rolling N-day window (suggest N=7) for a given vid+agency, there are >=K distinct transaction_ids (suggest K>=4) EACH strictly below a named control threshold T, whose sum >= T, AND the vendor is NOT in the agency's high-frequency cohort. Compute high-frequency cohort as vendors whose vid+agency pair has > P payments/year (e.g. > the 95th percentile of vendor_year_txns within that agency) so routine distributors (Cardinal Health, SHI, Axon, ADOT contractors) are excluded.
 2) control_threshold_usd: the actual Arizona delegated-purchasing / competitive-bid limit applicable to the agency+category+year (e.g. small-purchase / informal-bid ceilings). Needed because 'aggregating over a threshold' is meaningless without the real threshold; $250K/$1M used in prototyping are placeholders.
 3) distinct_invoice_count_in_window and just_below_threshold_share: how many of the clustered payments fall in a narrow band (e.g. 90-99.9%) just under the control threshold - a high share is the discriminating signal vs. random sub-threshold billing.
Without (1)-(3) the split angle is not safely deployable; the prototype confirmed it would flag legitimate high-volume vendors at scale (the Aztec/ZTE-class error this engagement must avoid).

GUARDRAIL NOTE on entitlement: AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS, TRANSFERS OUT, DEBT SERVICE excluded from the high-severity duplicate markers because Medicaid managed-care capitation and pension/fund paydowns are legitimate same-reference batches (verified: PSPRS $99M x10 same-day self-pay; Health Net / Mercy Care / DES Medicaid same-reference cross-period reissues).

markers.name: Discretionary procurement concentration (external vendor dominates an agency's discretionary spend)
markers.sql_predicate: amount >= 100000 AND vid IS NOT NULL AND vendor_share_of_agency_year >= 0.20 AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','EQUIPMENT','SUPPLIES AND MATERIALS','TRAVEL') AND NOT (payee = agency OR payee ILIKE 'STATE OF ARIZONA%' OR (payee ILIKE '%UNIVERSITY%' AND agency ILIKE '%UNIVERSITY%') OR (payee ILIKE '%COMMUNITY COLLEGE%' AND agency ILIKE '%COMMUNITY COLLEGE%')) AND NOT (regexp_matches(payee, '(^| )(U S BANK|BANK OF AMERICA|WELLS FARGO|JPMORGAN|BNY MELLON|NORTHERN TRUST)( |$|,)') OR payee ILIKE '%RETIREMENT SYSTEM%')
markers.applicable_years: FY2016-2018, FY2021, FY2023-2025. NOT FY2019/FY2020 (vid is NULL system-wide, so vendor_share_of_agency_year is NULL there and the predicate is correctly false; it never fires merely because the field is absent). FY2022 absent from mart. The 'vid IS NOT NULL' clause makes this degrade gracefully on thin years.
markers.rationale: An external (non-self, non-bank/pension) vendor that accounts for >=20% of an agency's TOTAL annual spend, and is paid out of a discretionary procurement category, is the classic sole-source / single-bid lead: the kind of relationship where you would pull the contract file and confirm the award was competed. The 20% floor is deliberately below the entitlement-dominated 50%+ band because, structurally, vendor_share_of_agency_year is measured against the whole agency-year (all categories), so even a true sole-source procurement vendor in a large diverse agency rarely crosses 50%. At 20%, restricted to discretionary categories and external payees, the population is 629 rows / 0.205% of the >=100K pop and the leads are textbook: NAPHCARE INC (sole inmate-healthcare contractor, Dept of Corrections, ~$30M/txn, 20-21% of agency), ASHBRITT INC (no-bid border-fence/disaster contractor, DEMA, 36%), WILLMENG CONSTRUCTION (Legislative Council capitol improvements, 46%), OFF MADISON AVE (Office of Tourism ad agency, 34%). These warrant confirmation that the procurement was competitively bid, not accusations.
markers.guardrails: (1) Entitlement/scheduled categories excluded by construction (only discretionary category1 values included) per the auditor risk-weighting brief. (2) Self-payments / intra-governmental transfers suppressed: payee=agency, 'STATE OF ARIZONA*' (school-finance pass-throughs via Dept of Education), university-to-self, community-college-to-self -- these dominate the high-share band and are benign-by-construction. (3) Banks and pension systems suppressed via WORD-BOUNDARY regexp (anchored on '^| ' and a trailing space/comma/end), explicitly so 'BANNER HEALTH' does NOT match 'BANK' -- this is the Aztec/ZTE substring-trap class of error and was unit-tested (BANK OF AMERICA matches, BANNER HEALTH does not). (4) vid IS NOT NULL guarantees the marker cannot fire on FY2019/2020 or on any row whose vendor identity is unresolved, so it never penalizes a system-wide-absent field. (5) amount>=100000 keeps headline stats on the institutional-materiality population. Residual benign risk: a legitimate statewide-sole-provider in a tiny single-purpose commission (e.g. PETSMART CHARITIES at the Spay/Neuter Committee) can appear -- treat as dependency, confirm via contract, do not escalate.

examples.why: Sole contracted inmate-health-care provider; ~$30M single payment, 20% of the entire department's annual spend. Lead: confirm the statewide inmate-healthcare contract was competitively procured.

examples.agency: DEPT OF EMERGENCY AND MILITARY AFFAIRS
examples.why: Border-security-fence / disaster contractor; 36% of DEMA's FY2023 spend across many large payments. Lead: confirm competitive award vs. emergency no-bid for the border-fence program.
$12.5M single payment, 35% of the department's year, Water Banking appropriation. Lead: confirm scope/competition for a single dominant water-banking vendor.
46% of Legislative Council's year on Capitol Improvements. Lead: confirm the capitol-construction contract was bid, not directed.
Dominant advertising agency, 34% of Office of Tourism spend (Prop 202 statewide promotion). Lead: confirm the agency-of-record contract was competed and renewals re-bid.

markers.name: Agency dependency / single-vendor exposure (>=33% of total agency-year spend)
markers.sql_predicate: amount >= 100000 AND vid IS NOT NULL AND vendor_share_of_agency_year >= 0.33 AND category1 NOT IN ('AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS','TRANSFERS OUT','DEBT SERVICE') AND NOT (payee = agency OR payee ILIKE 'STATE OF ARIZONA%' OR (payee ILIKE '%UNIVERSITY%' AND agency ILIKE '%UNIVERSITY%') OR (payee ILIKE '%COMMUNITY COLLEGE%' AND agency ILIKE '%COMMUNITY COLLEGE%'))
markers.applicable_years: FY2016-2018, FY2021, FY2023-2025. NOT FY2019/FY2020 (vendor_share_of_agency_year NULL there because vid is NULL system-wide). FY2022 absent from mart.
markers.rationale: Lower-suspicion companion to marker 1: any external vendor commanding >=33% of an agency's full annual spend, in any non-entitlement category. This deliberately KEEPS banks, utilities and pension systems in scope because, per the brief, those are dependency/exposure signals that bondholders care about (concentration risk, continuity-of-operations risk) even though they are NOT irregularities. 552 rows / 0.18% of the >=100K pop, $621M exposure. Composition is a healthy mix of benign-by-design exposure (BANK OF AMERICA -> Lottery Commission, 34% -- the exact example named in the brief; U S BANK as trustee/escrow at Statewide Systems Processing, 74%) and the same genuine-procurement names surfaced in marker 1. Severity is intentionally lower (2): the action is 'note the dependency and confirm contractual basis', not 'investigate'.
markers.guardrails: (1) Entitlement categories (AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS, TRANSFERS OUT, DEBT SERVICE) excluded -- these go high for scheduled/benefit reasons (e.g. STATE OF ARIZONA school-finance disbursements through Dept of Education) and carry low per-txn suspicion. (2) Self-payment / intra-governmental suppression identical to marker 1. (3) Banks/pension/utilities INTENTIONALLY retained (not suppressed) and labeled dependency, with the lower severity weight encoding 'exposure not irregularity'. (4) vid IS NOT NULL prevents firing on thin years or unresolved vendors. (5) Because vendor_share is whole-agency, this is an exposure lens, not a procurement-competition lens -- do not read a high share alone as evidence of anything improper.

examples.why: Benign dependency (the brief's own example): banking/settlement provider is structurally 34% of Lottery spend. Flag as exposure/continuity risk, NOT irregularity.
Trustee/escrow bank at 74% of a processing entity's spend. Benign dependency; confirm fee schedule, do not escalate.

examples.agency: DEPT OF EMERGENCY AND MILITARY AFFAIRS
examples.why: Genuine concentration also caught here at the agency level (36%); cross-reference marker 1.
Media/advertising vendor at 40% of a small commission's spend. Lead: confirm the voter-education media contract was competed.
52% of the commission's year on outside services. Dependency on a single agency-of-record; confirm competitive basis.

markers.name: Appropriation-level near-monopoly (one vendor is the near-entirety of a specific appropriation)
markers.sql_predicate: amount >= 100000 AND vid IS NOT NULL AND vendor_approp_year_share >= 0.80 AND approp_year_total >= 250000 AND category1 NOT IN ('AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS','TRANSFERS OUT','DEBT SERVICE') AND NOT (payee = agency OR payee ILIKE 'STATE OF ARIZONA%')
markers.applicable_years: FY2016-2018, FY2021, FY2023-2025 once the feature exists (requires vid; NULL in FY2019/2020). FY2022 absent from mart.
markers.rationale: The family description explicitly calls for 'a vendor that is the near-entirety of an appropriation' as a distinct, sharper signal than agency-wide share. An appropriation is a far narrower budget unit than an agency, so a vendor at >=80% of a single appropriation_1_code's year is a much tighter sole-source signal than agency share and would surface directed/earmarked single-vendor appropriations that agency-level concentration dilutes away. This is a PLACEHOLDER predicate: the mart exposes approp_year_total and approp_year_txns but NOT a per-(appropriation_1_code, fiscal_year, vid) vendor total, so vendor_approp_year_share cannot be computed from existing columns. I could not run this predicate; the calibrated_count/exposure below are 0 to mark it as not-yet-runnable rather than a measured population.
markers.guardrails: Same self-pay / entitlement guardrails as markers 1-2; word-boundary matching for any future name-based suppression. approp_year_total>=250000 floor avoids trivially-small appropriations where any single payment is 100% by arithmetic. vid IS NOT NULL for thin-year safety. The feature must be aggregated at the (appropriation_1_code, fiscal_year, vid) grain -- NOT on appropriation_1_name (names are non-unique across agencies) -- to avoid cross-agency contamination.

examples.why: Illustrative of the pattern the proposed feature targets: a single vendor at 99% of the Spaying-and-Neutering appropriation. Surfaced today only because this tiny committee has one appropriation; the proposed vendor_approp_year_share would generalize this to large agencies whose appropriation-level monopolies are hidden by agency-wide aggregation.

specs.notes:
CALIBRATION REASONING. vendor_share_of_agency_year is populated exactly when vid IS NOT NULL (1:1) -- both are NULL for 42.7% of the >=100K pop and 100% NULL in FY2019/2020 -- so 'vid IS NOT NULL' is the single guardrail that makes every marker degrade gracefully on thin years and unresolved vendors. The raw >=50% share band is a trap: it contains only 6 distinct agencies and ~90% of its rows are self-payments / intra-governmental transfers (universities paying themselves, STATE OF ARIZONA school-finance pass-throughs via Dept of Education, ARIZONA COMMERCE AUTHORITY). I therefore did NOT use a high raw-share cut as the headline marker; instead marker 1 lowers the threshold to 20% but restricts to discretionary categories + external payees, which is where genuine sole-source procurement lives. Both runnable markers fire on ~0.2% of the >=100K pop (well under the 5% severe ceiling).

WORD-BOUNDARY / ZTE-CLASS GUARDRAIL. The bank/pension suppression uses an anchored regexp ('(^| )(...)( |$|,)') not a substring ILIKE, and I unit-tested that 'BANK OF AMERICA' matches while 'BANNER HEALTH' does not -- directly addressing the Aztec/ZTE $47M false-positive lesson. Any future name-based rule should use the same anchoring.

OVERLAP WITH OTHER FAMILIES. (a) vendor_first_appearance (new-vendor family): a vendor that is both new AND concentrated is a stronger compound lead -- recommend AND-combining markers 1/2 with vendor_first_appearance in the scoring layer rather than duplicating here. (b) Round-dollar and dup-signature families overlap on the same NAPHCARE/ASHBRITT payment streams; concentration should add, not double-count. (c) robust_z_in_agencycat (peer-outlier) frequently co-fires on the large concentrated payments (NAPHCARE ~$30M txns) -- treat as corroboration. (d) Self-payment suppression here is also reusable by the intra-governmental-transfer family.

FEATURES NEEDED BUT ABSENT FROM MART. (1) vendor_approp_year_share (and its denominator vendor_approp_year_total) at the (appropriation_1_code, fiscal_year, vid) grain -- required for marker 3 (appropriation near-monopoly). Use appropriation_1_code, never appropriation_1_name (names repeat across agencies). (2) A canonical 'is_intragovernmental' / 'is_self_payment' boolean (and ideally a benign-counterparty registry: trustee banks, PSPRS, statewide sole providers, universities) -- I approximated self-payment with payee=agency plus ILIKE rules, which is robust for the dominant cases but will miss DBA/name-variant self-payments and is the main residual benign-leakage risk. (3) vendor_agency_years_active (count of distinct years a vendor has held a given agency relationship) would let marker 1 distinguish a long-standing competed incumbent from a sudden single-year monopoly, sharpening severity. (4) A herfindahl/HHI of vendor shares per agency-year would give a cleaner agency-level concentration scalar than thresholding a single vendor's share.

CATEGORY RISK-WEIGHTING applied per brief: AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS, TRANSFERS OUT, DEBT SERVICE are excluded as scheduled/entitlement (low per-txn suspicion); PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES / OTHER OPERATING / CAPITAL OUTLAY / EQUIPMENT / SUPPLIES / TRAVEL are the discretionary-procurement scope for the severe marker 1.

specs.family: New / first-appearance vendor at high value

markers.name: New vendor immediately dominates an agency's spend (onboarding-control lead)
markers.sql_predicate: vid IS NOT NULL AND vendor_first_appearance AND fiscal_year > 2016 AND amount >= 100000 AND vendor_year_total >= 1000000 AND vendor_share_of_agency_year >= 0.10 AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT') AND NOT (upper(appropriation_1_name) LIKE '%LUMP SUM%')
markers.applicable_years: FY2017, FY2018, FY2021, FY2023, FY2024, FY2025. CANNOT run FY2019/FY2020 (vid NULL system-wide, so vendor_first_appearance/vendor_year_total/share are unreliable). FY2016 EXCLUDED by predicate (left-censored first year of data: 100% of vendors falsely flag as first-appearance). FY2022 absent from mart.
markers.rationale: A vendor that appears for the very first time AND in that same year already accounts for >=10% of an entire agency's annual spend (with a year total >=$1M) is the canonical new-vendor onboarding-control lead: big money flowing to an entity with no prior procurement track record, concentrated in one agency. Restricted to discretionary procurement categories where vendor selection is controllable, so the same dollar carries real scrutiny weight (unlike entitlement/scheduled spend). Calibrated to 0.16% of the >=$100K population - well under the <5% severe-marker bar - so it is highly discriminating. Real leads surfaced: AshBritt Inc (new in FY2023, immediately 36% / $194M of Dept of Emergency & Military Affairs on border-security work) and Active Resource Management LLC (new in FY2017, 35% of Dept of Water Resources in its first year).
markers.guardrails: (1) vid IS NOT NULL required so the marker NEVER fires in thin years FY2019/2020 where vendor identity is system-wide NULL. (2) fiscal_year>2016 excludes the left-censoring artifact: FY2016 is the first data year so every vendor reads as first-appearance (e.g. ASU paying itself $90M lump-sum mislabeled 'new'). (3) Entitlement/scheduled categories (AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS = Medicaid/benefits, TRANSFERS OUT, DEBT SERVICE, COST ALLOCATION) are excluded - a new Medicaid MCO like Bridgeway Health Solutions at $59M is a scheduled capitation, not a procurement red flag. (4) LUMP SUM appropriations excluded - these are intra-government university/agency operating transfers, not arm's-length vendor onboarding. (5) CAVEAT, confirm before escalating: FY2021 is inflated by the post-gap re-appearance effect (vendors untracked during the FY2019/2020 NULL-vid gap re-surface as 'first appearance'); FY2021 leads need an independent corroborant (prior-period spend, contract record) before being treated as truly new. (6) Benign-match suppression to verify on review: legitimate competitively-awarded new contracts, renamed/merged entities reusing a new vid, one-off capital grants to municipalities, and federal pass-through grantees. This is a LEAD WARRANTING REVIEW, not a finding; it names no wrongdoer. Any future name-based refinement must use word-boundary matching, never substring (cf. the Aztec/ZTE false-positive class).

examples.agency: DEPT OF EMERGENCY AND MILITARY AFFAIRS
examples.why: First-appearance vendor in FY2023 that immediately became 36% ($194.7M year-total) of the agency's spend on PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES under a Border Security Fence appropriation - new vendor, immediate concentration; confirm competitive award and onboarding controls.

New vendor in FY2017 reaching 35% of the agency's OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES in its first year ($12.5M year-total); single-agency concentration by a no-track-record LLC warrants vendor-vetting review.

examples.agency: DEPT OF EMERGENCY AND MILITARY AFFAIRS
examples.why: Second large FY2023 invoice to the same new vendor, reinforcing rapid spend ramp to a first-appearance contractor; confirm contract basis and deliverables.

markers.name: First-appearance vendor with a large first discretionary payment
markers.sql_predicate: vid IS NOT NULL AND vendor_first_appearance AND fiscal_year > 2016 AND amount >= 100000 AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT') AND NOT (upper(appropriation_1_name) LIKE '%LUMP SUM%')
markers.applicable_years: FY2017, FY2018, FY2021, FY2023, FY2024, FY2025. CANNOT run FY2019/FY2020 (vid NULL). FY2016 EXCLUDED (left-censoring). FY2022 absent.
markers.rationale: Broader moderate-severity net: any first-appearance vendor receiving a >=$100K discretionary-procurement payment. New vendor + immediate large money is a classic onboarding-control lead even without the agency-dominance test. Calibrated to 1.73% of the >=$100K population (moderate band, <15%). This is the volume layer that feeds review queues; the dominant-share marker above is the high-confidence subset. Real leads: CRE-WPL Estrella JV LLC ($18.5M freeway construction, JV/SPV naming) and SJW Land Company ($18.8M land acquisition).
markers.guardrails: Same structural guardrails as the dominant-share marker: vid IS NOT NULL (never fires thin years), fiscal_year>2016 (drops FY2016 left-censoring), entitlement categories excluded (AID/TRANSFERS OUT/DEBT SERVICE), LUMP SUM excluded. HEAVY CAVEAT: 73% of this marker's rows fall in FY2021, driven by the post-NULL-vid-gap re-appearance artifact rather than true newness - treat FY2021 hits as low-confidence pending an independent prior-spend check; the ~1,400 non-FY2021 rows are the trustworthy core. Expected benign matches to confirm-not-accuse: legitimately competed new awards, renamed/merged entities, one-off municipal capital grants, federal pass-through grantees. Lead-not-finding framing; no name substring matching (word-boundary only).

examples.why: First-appearance joint-venture entity (JV/SPV naming pattern) receiving $18.6M for freeway construction (CAPITAL OUTLAY) in FY2024; new construction JVs are common but the large first payment warrants confirming the underlying competitively-bid contract.

New vendor's $18.9M first payment for a land/right-of-way acquisition (CAPITAL OUTLAY, FEDERAL AID) in FY2017; large one-off real-property purchases to a first-seen entity merit appraisal/title review.

examples.payee: BLUE CROSS BLUE SHIELD OF AZ INC
examples.why: Reads as first-appearance in FY2021 ($27.7M, employee benefits program) - an example of the FY2021 post-gap artifact: a long-standing benefits administrator that only looks 'new' because of the FY2019/2020 vid gap; illustrates why FY2021 hits need prior-spend corroboration before escalation.

markers.name: Short-tenure vendor with concentrated large spend (catches re-appearing JV/SPV entities)
markers.sql_predicate: vid IS NOT NULL AND vendor_years_active <= 2 AND vendor_year_total >= 2000000 AND fiscal_year > 2016 AND amount >= 100000 AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT') AND NOT (upper(appropriation_1_name) LIKE '%LUMP SUM%')
markers.applicable_years: FY2017, FY2018, FY2021, FY2023, FY2024, FY2025. CANNOT run FY2019/FY2020 (vid/vendor features NULL). FY2016 EXCLUDED. FY2022 absent.
markers.rationale: Complements the first-appearance markers by using vendor_years_active <= 2 rather than the strict first-appearance flag, so it captures very-short-lived vendors that received a large year total (>=$2M) but whose literal first appearance was a prior year - exactly the profile of single-project joint ventures / special-purpose vehicles that spin up, take large money, and dissolve. Restricted to the most procurement-discretionary categories (PROFESSIONAL/OUTSIDE SERVICES, CAPITAL OUTLAY/EQUIPMENT). Calibrated to 0.29% of the >=$100K population (well under severe bar). Surfaces Coffman Ames Joint Venture ($34M over 2 active years on CAPITAL OUTLAY) - a JV the pure first-appearance marker misses.
markers.guardrails: vid IS NOT NULL (no thin-year firing), fiscal_year>2016 (no left-censoring), LUMP SUM excluded, entitlement categories already excluded by the narrow category list. Note: legitimate short-duration construction/engineering JVs are common in ADOT capital programs and are the dominant benign class here - the marker flags them as items to confirm (is this a real competitively-awarded JV with bonded principals?), never as findings. vendor_years_active is computed only over years where the vendor is observable; the FY2019/2020 gap can understate tenure, so a vendor reading '2 years active' may have been active longer - corroborate tenure before treating short tenure as suspicious. Lead-not-finding; word-boundary not substring for any name logic.

examples.why: Joint-venture entity active only 2 years with a $34.4M year-total on CAPITAL OUTLAY (FY2023); short-lived JV/SPV taking concentrated capital dollars warrants confirming the JV agreement, bonding, and competitive award - not captured by first-appearance flag alone.

examples.agency: DEPT OF EMERGENCY AND MILITARY AFFAIRS
examples.why: Single-year-active vendor with a $194.7M year-total on PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES (FY2023); extreme spend through a short-tenure vendor is a strong concentration lead worth onboarding/contract review.
One-year-active vendor with a $20M year-total on CAPITAL OUTLAY (FY2017); transient vendor receiving large capital/land payments merits title and appraisal corroboration.

specs.notes:
CALIBRATION & ARTIFACT FINDINGS (load-bearing): (1) FY2016 is the FIRST data year, so 100% of observable vendors (19,172 of 19,172 vid rows) carry vendor_first_appearance=true - a pure left-censoring artifact. ALL three markers exclude fiscal_year=2016. Verified that vendor_first_year = fiscal_year is exactly equivalent to vendor_first_appearance (perfect 1:1 overlap), so the boolean flag is the right anchor. (2) FY2021 is inflated by a post-gap re-appearance effect: vendors were untracked during FY2019/2020 (vid NULL system-wide), so many long-standing vendors re-surface as 'first appearance' in FY2021. FY2021 alone is 73% of Marker A's rows (3,886 of 5,291); the BCBS-of-AZ example (a clearly pre-existing benefits administrator reading as 'new' in FY2021) demonstrates the false-positive mechanism. Recommendation: keep FY2021 firing but down-weight / require an independent prior-spend corroborant. The trustworthy core of Marker A is the ~1,405 non-FY2021 rows.

GUARDRAIL RATIONALE: Entitlement categories dominate raw first-appearance volume - AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS is ~70% of first-appearance >=$100K spend ($28.5B), and these are Medicaid MCOs/benefits/grants (e.g. Bridgeway Health Solutions $59M AHCCCS capitation) that are scheduled, not discretionary. Excluding entitlement/scheduled categories (AID, TRANSFERS OUT, DEBT SERVICE, COST ALLOCATION) and LUMP SUM appropriations (intra-government university operating transfers, e.g. ASU/UofA) removes the largest benign-by-construction mass and is what takes the family from 12.1% of the population (37,153 raw first-appearances) down to the calibrated 0.16-1.73% bands.

OVERLAP: Marker A (broad, sev 3) is a superset of Marker B (dominant share, sev 5) - all 494 B rows are within A. Marker C (short-tenure, sev 4) overlaps A/B on first-appearance vendors but ADDS value by catching 2-year-active JV/SPV entities (e.g. Coffman Ames Joint Venture) whose literal first appearance was a prior year, which the first-appearance flag misses. In a composite score, treat B as the high-confidence escalation of A, and C as an orthogonal short-tenure-concentration signal. Suggested composite: fire A as the base lead, +score if B (dominance) or C (short-tenure concentration) also fire, and corroborate with round-number flags (only 2.4% of Marker A rows are round_100k/round_1m, so round-number co-occurrence is a genuine additional signal, not baseline noise).

FEATURES NEEDED BUT ABSENT FROM MART (would sharpen this family, all currently approximated): (a) a vendor_observable_first_year that accounts for the FY2019/2020 gap, to distinguish true new vendors from post-gap re-appearances - the single biggest precision improvement available; the current vendor_first_appearance/vendor_first_year cannot see across the gap. (b) entity-type / SOS-registration-age flag (LLC/JV/SPV vs established corp vs municipality vs federal grantee) to auto-suppress the benign municipal-grant and federal-pass-through classes the guardrails currently can only describe. (c) a contract-existence flag (contract_number is only ~2-6% populated) - presence of a competitively-awarded contract record would materially de-risk a new-vendor lead. (d) vendor name-normalization with WORD-BOUNDARY tokenization for any future watchlist/affiliation matching - explicitly required to avoid the Aztec/ZTE substring false-positive class. All predicates above were executed against mart/hv_base.parquet and return the stated calibrated counts.

markers.name: High-value posting in adjustment period 13 (top-side closing entry)
markers.sql_predicate: amount >= 100000 AND fp_canon = 13
markers.applicable_years: FY2016-2018, FY2021, FY2023-2025 (fp_canon present). NOT FY2019/2020 (fp_canon NULL system-wide) and FY2022 (excluded from mart). Within these years the marker self-restricts: rows with fp_canon NULL never fire, so it degrades gracefully.
markers.rationale: fp_canon=13 is the post-June-close adjustment period used for year-end true-ups and top-side entries. Only 0.16% of the >=$100K population posts here (486 rows, $2.93B), so any high-value item is a closing-entry lead worth confirming. Empirically 100% of these are INTERNAL/JOURNAL VOUCHER/NULL rail with NULL or 'N/A' payee (370 INTERNAL, 114 NULL, 2 JV; zero ACH/WARRANT external payments) — they are book entries, exactly the population where management override and reclassification risk concentrates. This is the broad standalone timing flag; pair it with the discretionary sub-marker for sharper triage.
markers.guardrails: Item to confirm, not a finding: the dominant content is legitimate scheduled true-ups -- DEBT SERVICE ($571M, median $66.7M, clearly scheduled), AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS ($1.64B Medicaid/benefit accruals) and TRANSFERS OUT ($562M). These are expected-large entitlement/scheduled categories and should be DOWN-weighted (treat as informational, not severe) when reviewing. Never fires in FY19/20 because fp_canon IS NULL there. No name matching used, so no ZTE/Aztec-class substring risk. Do not penalize a row merely for NULL payee here -- NULL payee is the structural norm for period-13 book entries.

examples.why: $5.6M sales-commission accrual booked in adjustment period 13 on INTERNAL rail with no payee -- a closing true-up to confirm against the retailer commission schedule.
Round $3.0M 'Education for the Disadvantaged' booked in period 13, INTERNAL rail -- year-end grant reclassification to verify.
$1.64M judicial private-grants entry in adjustment period -- top-side closing entry warranting documentation review.

markers.name: Discretionary-procurement value posted in adjustment period 13
markers.sql_predicate: amount >= 100000 AND fp_canon = 13 AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT')
markers.applicable_years: FY2016-2018, FY2021, FY2023-2025 (fp_canon present). NOT FY2019/2020 (fp_canon NULL) or FY2022 (excluded). Self-restricts on fp_canon, so degrades gracefully in thin years.
markers.rationale: Sharpens the broad period-13 flag by removing scheduled/entitlement categories (DEBT SERVICE, AID, TRANSFERS OUT, COST ALLOCATION, PERSONAL SERVICES) and keeping only discretionary procurement, where a closing-period entry is least expected and carries the most scrutiny weight. Fires on just 0.063% of the >=$100K population (192 rows, $120.3M) -- a tight, high-discrimination set. Of these, 29 also carry robust_z>=10 (anomalously large within their agency x category peer group) and 13 are round 100K/1M, reinforcing the 'estimated/sweep entry' read. Reclassifying discretionary spend into the adjustment period is a classic period-shifting / use-it-or-lose-it move that should be confirmed against supporting invoices/accrual basis.
markers.guardrails: Item to confirm, not an accusation: legitimate year-end accruals for services delivered-not-yet-invoiced and IT/automation true-ups appear here (e.g. court 'AUTOMATION', 'IBM LEGACY PROJECT'). Reviewer should confirm an accrual basis and that goods/services were received by June 30. Entitlement categories are excluded by construction so the marker never penalizes scheduled benefit spending. Never fires FY19/20. No substring name matching. NULL/'N/A' payee is structurally normal for these book entries and must not itself raise severity.

examples.why: $5.37M 'Non Contract Medication' (OTHER OPERATING) posted in adjustment period -- discretionary procurement reclassified at close; confirm receipt/accrual basis.
Round $2.0M 'Statewide Assessment Supplemental' (PROFESSIONAL SERVICES) in period 13 -- round-dollar discretionary closing entry to verify.
$1.5M 'IBM Legacy Project' CAPITAL EQUIPMENT on JOURNAL VOUCHER in adjustment period -- capital reclassification at year-end warranting review.

markers.name: Year-end (June) round-dollar discretionary spend (use-it-or-lose-it signature)
markers.sql_predicate: amount >= 100000 AND fp_canon = 12 AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','EMPLOYEE RELATED EXPENDITURES') AND (is_round_100k OR is_round_1m)
markers.applicable_years: FY2016-2018, FY2021, FY2023-2025 (fp_canon present). NOT FY2019/2020 (fp_canon NULL) or FY2022 (excluded). Self-restricts on fp_canon.
markers.rationale: A flat 'posted in June' flag is too broad (June = 10.6% of the >=$100K pop, exceeding the <15% moderate threshold). This marker isolates the disproportionate signal: round-dollar (exactly $X00,000 or $Xm) DISCRETIONARY spend in the final budget period. Round-number rate in June discretionary is 2.86% vs 1.91% in other months -- a 1.5x year-end lift consistent with appropriation-sweeping / estimated commitments before lapse. Fires on only 0.062% of the population (191 rows, $1.18B). The round amount is the discriminator that separates a true 'use-it-or-lose-it' estimate from ordinary invoice-driven June activity. Reviewer confirms whether the round figure reflects a real deliverable or a budget-balance estimate.
markers.guardrails: Item to confirm, not a finding: round-dollar June payments are frequently legitimate (grant disbursements, contract milestone draws, intergovernmental transfers). Notable benign-by-pattern subset: PSPRS booking ten identical round $99M 'Unfunded Liability Reduction' self-payments (agency=payee) in June FY2021 -- scheduled pension contributions, low suspicion despite firing. Excludes entitlement/scheduled categories (AID, DEBT SERVICE, TRANSFERS OUT). To prioritize the cleanest external leads, reviewers may add 'AND payee <> agency AND vid IS NOT NULL' (filters intra-government and thin-year rows). Never fires FY19/20. No substring name matching -- only exact category-set and numeric round flags, so no ZTE/Aztec-class false positives.

examples.why: Round $23.0M PROFESSIONAL SERVICES (federal grants) to ASU posted in June FY2021 -- external round-dollar year-end payment; confirm it maps to a defined deliverable, not a lapse-driven estimate.
Round $4.0M June payment to a healthcare vendor -- year-end round-dollar procurement draw to verify against contract milestones.
Round $3.0M IT services to CGI in June FY2024 -- round-figure year-end spend warranting confirmation of work performed vs. budget-balance estimate.

specs.notes:
CALIBRATION: All headline stats restricted to amount>=100000 (the 306,604-row >=$100K population per the brief). Counts: p13_all=486 (0.159%), p13_discretionary=192 (0.063%), june_round_disc=191 (0.062%) -- all well under the <5% severe threshold, so all three are clean, discriminating signals rather than noise. fp_canon is present in FY2016-2018, 2021, 2023-2025; NULL system-wide in FY2019/2020; FY2022 absent from mart. Every predicate self-restricts on fp_canon so none can fire in a thin year (graceful degradation requirement satisfied).

KEY EMPIRICAL FINDING: There are ZERO external-vendor (ACH/WARRANT) payments in adjustment period 13 at >=$100K -- every period-13 high-value row is INTERNAL/JV/NULL rail with NULL or 'N/A' payee. Period-13 is therefore intrinsically a book-entry/top-side phenomenon, so 'real external payee' cannot be used to sharpen it; I sharpened via discretionary category instead (p13_discretionary). Conversely, the June marker DOES contain real external vendors (ASU, NaphCare, Centurion, CGI, Pulice), so the round-amount + discretionary-category filter is the right discriminator there.

GUARDRAIL DESIGN: Entitlement/scheduled categories (AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS, DEBT SERVICE, TRANSFERS OUT) are excluded from the two sharpened markers per the expected-large vs discretionary risk-weighting; they dominate raw period-13 dollars ($2.77B of the $2.93B) and are benign-by-construction year-end true-ups. NULL/'N/A' payee is treated as structurally normal (not penalized) for closing entries. No marker uses any name/substring matching, so the ZTE/Aztec false-positive class is impossible here -- predicates are purely categorical, numeric-round-flag, and fiscal-period based.

OVERLAP WITH OTHER FAMILIES: (1) p13_discretionary overlaps the manual-rail family -- 100% of period-13 rows are INTERNAL/JV (which equals the 'manual rail' 11.5% baseline driver); dedupe so a row firing here is not double-counted as a generic manual-rail flag. (2) june_round_disc overlaps the round-number family (is_round_100k/is_round_1m, baselines 1.8%/0.6%) and the duplicate family (the PSPRS $99M repeats would also trip dup_signature/dup_diffday); compose with OR and de-duplicate exposure. (3) p13_discretionary overlaps the peer-anomaly family (29 of 192 have robust_z>=10) -- a row firing both is a stronger composite lead.

FEATURES NEEDED BUT ABSENT FROM MART (returned via placeholder, not used in the running predicates above): A true 'disproportionate final-period concentration' signal would need a per-vendor-year or per-appropriation-year monthly distribution -- e.g. a column like june_share_of_vendor_year (fraction of a vendor's annual dollars posted in fp_canon=12). The mart has vendor_year_total and approp_year_total but no within-year monthly/period breakdown, so I approximated 'use-it-or-lose-it' with the round-amount + June + discretionary composite. If added, the ideal predicate would be: amount>=100000 AND fp_canon=12 AND june_share_of_appropriation_year >= 0.5 AND approp_year_txns >= 4 (an appropriation that dumps >=half its annual high-value dollars in June across multiple transactions). Recommend adding june_share_of_appropriation_year and june_share_of_vendor_year to the mart to upgrade marker 3 from a proxy to a direct concentration measure.

markers.name: JOURNAL VOUCHER / INTERNAL rail on discretionary procurement
markers.sql_predicate: payment_method IN ('JOURNAL VOUCHER','INTERNAL') AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT')
markers.applicable_years: FY2016-2018, FY2021, FY2023-2025, plus FY2019-2020 (payment_method is populated system-wide in every year, including the thin years; this family does NOT depend on appr_type_canon/vid/fp_canon, so it degrades gracefully and runs in FY19/20).
markers.rationale: JOURNAL VOUCHER and INTERNAL bypass the normal ACH accounts-payable workflow and are manually-keyed ledger entries. On scheduled/entitlement spend (Medicaid AID, inter-fund TRANSFERS OUT, DEBT SERVICE) such entries are routine and benign. But on DISCRETIONARY procurement (professional/outside services, other operating, capital outlay/equipment) a manual rail at >=$100K means a high-value purchase was settled outside the standard vendor-AP control path - a classic override/segregation-of-duties lead warranting review. Calibrates to 3.29% of the >=$100K population, appropriate for a severe standalone signal. Examples include a $92M border-fence equipment buy posted INTERNAL with payee 'N/A' and a $35M ADOT freeway-construction capital outlay posted as a JOURNAL VOUCHER with a null payee - items an auditor would want to confirm against source documentation.
markers.guardrails: Entitlement/scheduled categories are EXCLUDED by construction: the predicate whitelists only the four discretionary procurement category1 values, so it never fires on TRANSFERS OUT (10,013 INTERNAL rows / $20.2B), AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS, DEBT SERVICE, or COST ALLOCATION AND INDIRECT COSTS - exactly the inter-fund JV/INTERNAL traffic the family caveat warns about. No name/substring matching is involved (rail + category enums only), so the ZTE/Aztec false-positive class cannot occur. Produces leads to confirm, not findings. Note OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES still contains some scheduled COP debt-service paydowns labeled under that category (e.g. DOA 'COP DEBT SERVICE' INTERNAL entries); reviewers should de-prioritize rows whose appropriation_1_name contains a debt-service token - this is a refinement, not a fraud determination.

examples.agency: DEPT OF EMERGENCY AND MILITARY AFFAIRS
examples.why: $92M of CAPITAL EQUIPMENT (Border Security Fence and Technologies) settled on the INTERNAL rail with no named payee - a very large discretionary capital buy outside the standard AP/ACH path; confirm vendor and procurement authority.
$35M CAPITAL OUTLAY (freeway construction, M-RARF) posted as a JOURNAL VOUCHER with a null payee - manual ledger entry for a large capital project; confirm supporting documentation.

$36M OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES via INTERNAL with null appropriation and no payee (FY2020) - high-value manual entry to confirm; reviewer should check whether it is a scheduled COP debt paydown before escalating.

markers.name: Any JOURNAL VOUCHER / INTERNAL rail outside entitlement categories (broad sweep)
markers.sql_predicate: payment_method IN ('JOURNAL VOUCHER','INTERNAL') AND category1 NOT IN ('TRANSFERS OUT','AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS','DEBT SERVICE','COST ALLOCATION AND INDIRECT COSTS')
markers.applicable_years: All in-scope years FY2016-2025 (payment_method populated system-wide every year; runs in thin FY2019-2020).
markers.rationale: Broader companion to the discretionary marker: captures ALL manual-rail (JV/INTERNAL) high-value spend that is not a scheduled entitlement, including PERSONAL SERVICES, EMPLOYEE RELATED EXPENDITURES, TRAVEL and FOOD in addition to the core procurement categories. Useful as a moderate-weight net so genuinely-manual operational spend in categories outside the strict procurement whitelist is still surfaced. Calibrates to 4.49% of the >=$100K population - within the <15% moderate band, and tighter than the raw 11.5% manual-rail baseline because entitlements are removed.
markers.guardrails: Excludes the four scheduled/entitlement categories (TRANSFERS OUT, AID, DEBT SERVICE, COST ALLOCATION AND INDIRECT COSTS) where JV/INTERNAL is the normal mechanism - this is the explicit inter-fund-transfer guardrail from the family brief. Pure enum logic, no name matching, so no substring false-positive risk. Lower severity (3) than the discretionary marker because PERSONAL SERVICES / EMPLOYEE RELATED / TRAVEL manual entries are more often legitimate payroll-adjacent reclassifications; treat as a corroborating signal, not standalone. Leads to confirm only.

examples.agency: DEPT OF EMERGENCY AND MILITARY AFFAIRS
examples.why: Same border-fence CAPITAL EQUIPMENT INTERNAL entry - also caught by the broad net; high-value manual rail outside entitlements.
$60M OTHER OPERATING via INTERNAL (recurring across FY16-21) - large repeating manual entry to confirm as a scheduled COP obligation vs. a discretionary item.
$35M freeway-construction JOURNAL VOUCHER - manual capital entry outside entitlement categories.

markers.name: Unclassified (NULL) or WARRANT rail on discretionary procurement
markers.sql_predicate: (payment_method IS NULL OR payment_method = 'WARRANT') AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT')
markers.applicable_years: All in-scope years FY2016-2025. NULL payment_method runs ~15-18% in EVERY year (it is a real 'rail not classified' state, NOT a system-wide-absent field), so this is a genuine signal in all years including the thin ones - it is not penalizing a year for an absent field.
markers.rationale: WARRANT (paper-warrant) and unclassified-rail (NULL payment_method) high-value items also sit off the standard ACH path. On discretionary procurement these are weaker but real leads: paper warrants for large purchases are unusual and NULL-rail means the settlement channel was never recorded. Calibrates to 5.78% of the >=$100K population. Lower severity (2) because NULL/WARRANT are far noisier than JV/INTERNAL and a sizeable share are legitimate (e.g. real-property closings paid by warrant to title companies).
markers.guardrails: Restricted to the discretionary procurement categories so the very large NULL-rail entitlement traffic (AID 28,588 rows / $59B, TRANSFERS 5,876 rows / $21.7B) is excluded - those would otherwise swamp the marker. Because NULL is common and partly benign, this marker is intended only as a low-weight corroborator that should be combined with another red flag (e.g. vendor_first_appearance, round_100k, robust_z>=10) before escalation; do NOT treat a NULL rail alone as suspicious. No name matching, so no substring false-positive risk. Leads to confirm only.
