examples.why: $28M CAPITAL OUTLAY paid by WARRANT to a title agency - a real-property closing; plausibly legitimate but a paper-warrant high-value item worth confirming against the land-acquisition file.
$19M CAPITAL OUTLAY by WARRANT for land - non-ACH high-value settlement; confirm appraisal/closing documentation.
Large OTHER OPERATING discretionary item with no recorded rail/payee - confirm the settlement channel.

specs.notes:
CALIBRATION: All three predicates were run against mart/hv_base.parquet restricted to amount>=100000 (population = 306,604). Raw rail mix in that population: ACH 162,489; NULL 48,937; INTERNAL 27,802; PAYMENT 25,445; WARRANT 25,298; COST ALLOCATION 9,297; JOURNAL VOUCHER 7,336. Raw manual-rail (JV+INTERNAL) baseline = 35,138 = 11.5% (matches the brief's stated 11.5% manual-rail baseline). Filtering to non-entitlement brings it to 4.49% (marker B); filtering to the strict discretionary whitelist brings it to 3.29% (marker A).

GUARDRAIL DETAIL / GOTCHA: category1 uses the value 'COST ALLOCATION AND INDIRECT COSTS', NOT 'COST ALLOCATION' (which is only a payment_method value). The exclusion lists use the correct category1 spelling. The single biggest benign block the brief warns about - INTERNAL on inter-fund TRANSFERS OUT - is 10,013 rows / $20.2B and is fully excluded by all three markers.

THIN-YEAR BEHAVIOR: payment_method is populated system-wide in every fiscal year including FY2019 and FY2020 (FY19 has_rail 23,337/28,360; FY20 25,920/31,822). This family therefore does NOT need a 'vid IS NOT NULL' or 'appr_type_canon IS NOT NULL' guard - it does not depend on any of the three fields that go NULL in the thin years. I deliberately did NOT add such a guard because it would needlessly suppress valid FY19/20 leads. The NULL-rail share (~15-18%) is stable across all years, confirming NULL is a genuine 'unrecorded channel' state rather than an absent-field artifact - so marker C does not penalize any year for a system-wide gap.

OVERLAP WITH OTHER FAMILIES: (1) NULL/empty payee ('N/A', SQL NULL) co-occurs heavily with these manual-rail items - a 'missing payee' marker family would overlap strongly with markers A and C and should be combined, not double-counted. (2) Period-13 / fp_canon=13 adjustment entries are frequently posted as JOURNAL VOUCHER; a period-13 marker overlaps with marker A/B but fp_canon is NULL in FY19/20 so that family cannot corroborate there. (3) round-dollar and robust_z markers are the recommended corroborators for the low-weight marker C before escalation.

FEATURES NEEDED BUT ABSENT FROM MART: A cleaner severity split would benefit from (a) a boolean is_debt_service_appropriation flag derived from appropriation_1_name (to demote the COP/debt-paydown rows that currently sit in OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES and INTERNAL - I left these in marker A but flagged them in guardrails because no such column exists), and (b) a payee_is_missing boolean (payee IS NULL OR payee IN ('N/A','NULL','MISCPAYVEND')) to let manual-rail-with-no-payee be scored higher. Both can be added as derived columns; the current predicates use only existing hv_base columns and are valid DuckDB boolean expressions as run.

markers.name: Extreme peer-outlier on discretionary spend (robust-z within agency x category)
markers.sql_predicate: amount >= 100000 AND robust_z_in_agencycat >= 20 AND ac_n >= 8 AND category1 NOT IN ('AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS','TRANSFERS OUT','DEBT SERVICE','COST ALLOCATION AND INDIRECT COSTS','PERSONAL SERVICES','EMPLOYEE RELATED EXPENDITURES')
markers.applicable_years: FY2016-2025 (excl. FY2022 not in mart). Fires in ALL available years including the thin FY2019/2020: robust_z_in_agencycat is derived from amount vs the agency x category1 median/IQR and does NOT depend on vid, appr_type_canon, or fp_canon, so it degrades gracefully where those are NULL. Verified 181-375 hits/yr with no degenerate year.
markers.rationale: robust_z_in_agencycat = (amount - median)/(IQR/1.349) within each agency x category1 peer set. A z >= 20 means the line item is ~20 robust-SDs above its own agency's typical spend for that category - i.e. the amount dwarfs the peer norm. The baseline says robust_z>=10 is 14.4% of the >=$100K pop, far too broad (it is mostly the benign entitlement tail). Restricting to z>=20 AND removing scheduled/entitlement categories isolates the genuine procurement outliers: 2,278 rows (0.74% of the 306,604 >=$100K pop), $15.6B exposure. The cutoff was tuned by sweeping z in {15,20,25,30,40} on the discretionary subset (3,504 / 2,279 / 1,709 / 1,355 / 843) and choosing 20 as the knee that stays <1% of pop while keeping enough volume to be a usable lead queue.
markers.guardrails: (1) EXCLUDES entitlement / scheduled categories (AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS = AHCCCS/Medicaid capitation, TRANSFERS OUT, DEBT SERVICE) plus COST ALLOCATION, PERSONAL SERVICES and EMPLOYEE RELATED - these are the documented mega-program benign outliers (AHCCCS Prop 204 capitation hit z up to 3,864 for benign reasons; 33,108 of 44,158 z>=10 rows are entitlement). (2) Requires ac_n >= 8 so z is computed against a stable peer group, not 2-3 rows (only 118 rows in pop have ac_n<8, so this barely costs coverage but prevents unstable-denominator artifacts). (3) Pure-amount signal, so no name-matching false-positive class here; the Aztec/ZTE substring lesson does not apply but is respected by avoiding any LIKE on payee. (4) This is a LEAD, not a finding: a high z legitimately occurs for one-off emergency procurement (e.g. AshBritt border-fence) - the marker says 'confirm the contracting basis,' it does not assert impropriety.

examples.agency: DEPT OF EMERGENCY AND MILITARY AFFAIRS
examples.why: robust_z=839.8 within DEMA x Professional Services; $28.7M to a single disaster/logistics vendor under BORDER SECURITY FENCE appropriation - ~840 robust-SDs above DEMA's normal professional-services line. Review the emergency-contracting basis and competitive process.

robust_z=880.4 within ADOT x Professional Services under HIGHWAY USER REVENUE; largest single professional-services line for the agency-category, and payee is unnamed - confirm counterparty and scope.

robust_z=490.2 within Lottery x Other Operating; $92M single line to a bank, round-ish, NULL appropriation - confirm whether this is a prize-pool/banking sweep (likely benign mechanics) vs a procurement item.
robust_z=444.5 within ADC x Other Operating under PRIVATE PRISON COP; $17.5M outlier with masked payee - confirm the private-prison vendor identity and the per-diem basis.

markers.name: Single third-party vendor dominates an agency's discretionary year (sole-source concentration / spike proxy)
markers.sql_predicate: amount >= 100000 AND vid IS NOT NULL AND vendor_share_of_agency_year >= 0.33 AND vendor_agency_year_total >= 1000000 AND category1 NOT IN ('AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS','TRANSFERS OUT','DEBT SERVICE','COST ALLOCATION AND INDIRECT COSTS') AND payee IS NOT NULL AND payee NOT IN ('N/A','STATE OF ARIZONA') AND NOT (upper(regexp_replace(payee,'[^A-Za-z0-9]','','g')) LIKE upper(regexp_replace(agency,'[^A-Za-z0-9]','','g')) || '%') AND NOT (upper(regexp_replace(agency,'[^A-Za-z0-9]','','g')) LIKE upper(regexp_replace(payee,'[^A-Za-z0-9]','','g')) || '%')
markers.applicable_years: FY2016-2018, FY2021, FY2023-2025 ONLY. CANNOT run in FY2019/FY2020 because vid is NULL system-wide there; the predicate's vid IS NOT NULL clause makes it self-suppress in thin years (it will simply not fire rather than mis-fire). Also note vid is only ~24-48% populated even in good years (42.7% of pop has NULL vid), so this marker covers the vid-identified subset and is deliberately conservative.
markers.rationale: vendor_share_of_agency_year is the fraction of an agency's total spend in a fiscal year captured by one vendor. A single third-party vendor taking >=1/3 of an agency's discretionary year (with >= $1M absolute to that vendor) approximates the requested year-over-year / concentration-spike angle: it surfaces sole-source-like dependence and large vendor ramps. Baseline vendor_share>=50% is 3.1% of pop, but ~80% of that is intra-government self-payment (agency paying itself - e.g. ASU->ASU, UofA Financial Services). After removing self-payments the share>=50% population collapses to 8 genuine third-party rows; loosening to share>=0.33 (vendor is the dominant single counterparty, 1/3+ of the year) yields 594 rows (0.19% of the >=$100K pop), $952.5M exposure - a tight, high-precision lead set.
markers.guardrails: (1) SELF-PAYMENT GUARDRAIL using word/character-normalized PREFIX matching, not substring: strips non-alphanumerics and checks whether payee starts-with agency (or vice-versa). This was essential - a naive payee<>agency exact check leaked 'UNIVERSITY OF ARIZONA (FINANCIAL SERVICES OFFICE)' as if it were a third party. This is the exact discipline lesson from the Aztec/ZTE incident applied in reverse: match on normalized boundaries, never raw substrings. (2) Explicitly excludes the intra-gov sinks 'STATE OF ARIZONA' and masked 'N/A' payees. (3) vid IS NOT NULL guarantees it never fires in thin FY2019/2020 and never penalizes a row for a system-wide-absent field. (4) Excludes entitlement/scheduled categories where one payee legitimately dominates (Medicaid MCOs, debt trustees, transfer recipients). (5) vendor_agency_year_total >= $1M floor prevents firing on tiny agencies where one $100K invoice is mechanically 100% of a trivial budget. (6) Severity 3 (moderate) not 4: high concentration is common and often legitimate (a genuine sole authorized provider); the marker says 'confirm sole-source justification / verify this is not a split-driver,' it is a context flag, not a standalone red flag.

examples.why: vendor_share=0.74 - US Bank captured ~74% of this unit's discretionary year; new-vendor-first-appearance=true in FY2016. Confirm whether this is a contracted banking/p-card program (likely benign) vs unreviewed concentration.

vendor_share=0.52 - one advertising/communications firm took >half the Commission's professional-services year, first appearance FY2016. Review the procurement/competitive basis for a sole dominant media vendor at an elections body.

vendor_share=0.46 across multiple FY2021 line items (Professional Services + Capital Outlay) to a single quasi-public counterparty - confirm the interagency-agreement basis and that this is not a pass-through obscuring ultimate recipients.

specs.notes:
OVERLAP: The two markers are nearly disjoint - only 151 rows satisfy both the z>=20 discretionary predicate AND the vendor-dominance predicate (before the M2 self-pay/share refinement; under the final M2 share>=0.33 the practical overlap is smaller). They capture different angles: M1 = a single line item is huge vs its category peers (amount-anomaly); M2 = a vendor relationship dominates an agency-year (relationship/concentration-anomaly). They should be combined with OR and the row carries the max severity, with a small bonus if BOTH fire (corroborated outlier).

CALIBRATION REASONING: Headline stats restricted to amount>=100000 (306,604-row pop). M1 = 2,278 rows (0.74%), $15.55B. M2 = 594 rows (0.19%), $952.5M. Both comfortably under the <5% severe-marker rule of thumb. M1's z cutoff (20) was chosen from a sweep {15,20,25,30,40} on the non-entitlement subset; 20 is the knee keeping it <1% of pop. M2's share cutoff (0.33) was chosen after a sweep {0.50:8, 0.40:25, 0.33:594, 0.25:1587} on the self-pay-cleaned third-party subset; 0.33 is the lowest threshold that stays a tight high-precision queue (<0.2% of pop) while giving usable volume - 0.50 was too sparse (8 rows) to be a standalone marker once self-payments were stripped.

KEY GUARDRAIL FINDING (benign-by-construction artifacts caught): (a) The entitlement tail is the dominant benign-outlier class for robust_z - AHCCCS Prop 204 capitation reaches z up to 3,864 legitimately; 33,108 of 44,158 z>=10 rows are entitlement. Excluding those categories is what makes M1 discriminating. (b) For M2, ~80% of high vendor_share rows are intra-government self-payments (agency paying a decorated version of its own name, e.g. ASU->ASU, 'UNIVERSITY OF ARIZONA (FINANCIAL SERVICES OFFICE)'->UNIVERSITY OF ARIZONA). A normalized-prefix (boundary, not substring) self-payment filter was required and removes 1,942 of 2,434 share>=50% rows. This directly honors the Aztec/ZTE word-boundary discipline.

FEATURES NEEDED BUT ABSENT FROM MART (returned predicates use only existing columns, but these would strengthen the family): (1) A true year-over-year vendor-spend delta (vendor_year_total this FY vs prior FY for the same vid) would let M2 flag genuine spikes/ramps rather than static concentration; the mart has vendor_year_total and vendor_first_year/vendor_years_active but not a prior-year-total column, so I approximated the spike via concentration share. Suggested column: vendor_prior_year_total (DECIMAL) and vendor_yoy_growth (double). (2) An explicit is_self_payment / is_intragovernmental boolean precomputed from a normalized agency-vs-payee/entity comparison would make the M2 guardrail robust and reusable rather than embedding the regexp_replace prefix logic inline; suggested column: is_self_or_intragov (bool). (3) A peer-group fiscal-year-scoped robust_z (robust_z_in_agencycat_year) would prevent a structural change across years from inflating z; current robust_z appears pooled across years within agency x category1.

FRAMING: Both markers produce items-to-confirm, never findings. Named payees in examples (AshBritt, US Bank, R&R Partners, Commerce Authority) are reproduced solely as the data shows them for traceability of the lead; nothing here asserts wrongdoing by any party - each example's 'why' states the specific thing an auditor should confirm (emergency-contracting basis, sole-source justification, interagency-agreement basis, prize-pool mechanics).

markers.name: Generic/placeholder payee on a discretionary procurement item
markers.sql_predicate: amount >= 100000 AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT') AND (payee IN ('N/A','MISCELLANEOUS VENDOR','MISCELLANEOUS CUSTOMER') OR payee IS NULL OR regexp_matches(upper(payee), '(^| )(PCARD|FUEL) VENDOR( |$)'))
markers.applicable_years: FY2016-2018, FY2021, FY2023-2025 (all non-thin years). Also runs in FY2019-2020 because it depends only on payee+category1, both of which are populated in thin years; however vid/appr_type context for triage is unavailable there.
markers.rationale: A six-figure discretionary-procurement payment (professional services, capital outlay/equipment, other operating) with no identifiable counterparty is a core accountability gap: oversight cannot verify who was paid or whether the vendor was eligible/competitively selected. The generic tokens are an exact allow-list curated from the data: 'N/A' (11,742 discretionary rows), actual NULL payee (3,139), 'MISCELLANEOUS VENDOR'/'MISCELLANEOUS CUSTOMER', and the system's own 'PCARD VENDOR xxx'/'FUEL VENDOR xxx' purchase-card placeholders (a $100K+ p-card line with no named merchant is itself a control concern). Fires on 15,047 rows / $8.92B = 4.91% of the >=$100K population, just under the 5% severe threshold.
markers.guardrails: WORD-BOUNDARY / EXACT-MATCH ONLY to avoid the ZTE/Aztec false-positive class: payee tokens are matched by exact equality ('N/A' etc.) or regex with explicit word boundaries '(^| )(PCARD|FUEL) VENDOR( |$)' -- NEVER a substring LIKE '%TEMP%' (which spuriously hit CITY OF TEMPE, TEMPLE EMANUEL, CONTEMPORARY SERVICES). DELIBERATELY EXCLUDES the literal string payee 'NULL' and 'NAME REDACTED': those are concentrated in AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS / TRANSFERS OUT / PERSONAL SERVICES (privacy-redacted benefit recipients and payroll) and are benign-by-construction, not procurement gaps. Restricting category1 to discretionary procurement excludes entitlement/scheduled spend where a missing individual name is expected. No vid/appr_type/fp dependence, so it degrades gracefully and never penalizes a thin-year field.

examples.agency: DEPT OF EMERGENCY AND MILITARY AFFAIRS
examples.why: $92M CAPITAL EQUIPMENT line 'BORDER SECURITY FENCE AND TECHNOLOGIES' on an INTERNAL rail with payee 'N/A' -- no identifiable vendor on a very large discretionary capital purchase; confirm counterparty and procurement basis.
$35M CAPITAL OUTLAY ('M-RARF CONSTRUCT FREEWAYS') via JOURNAL VOUCHER with a NULL payee -- large construction spend with no recorded payee; item to confirm.
$34M PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES ('HEPATITIS C TREATMENT RX') with payee 'N/A' -- large professional/pharma services payment with no named vendor; confirm payee identity.

$132K OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES booked to the generic 'PCARD VENDOR PSA' purchase-card placeholder -- six-figure p-card line with no named merchant; confirm the actual vendor behind the aggregated p-card charge.

markers.name: Triple accountability gap: no vendor id + manual/internal rail + no payee on discretionary spend
markers.sql_predicate: amount >= 100000 AND fiscal_year NOT IN (2019,2020) AND vid IS NULL AND payment_method IN ('JOURNAL VOUCHER','INTERNAL') AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT')
markers.applicable_years: FY2016-2018, FY2021, FY2023-2025 ONLY. EXPLICITLY EXCLUDES FY2019-2020 because vid is NULL system-wide there -- firing on a thin year would be a pure data-quality artifact, not a real gap.
markers.rationale: This is the higher-severity intersection: a large discretionary expenditure that simultaneously (1) has no real vendor id (vid NULL) in a year where vid IS otherwise populated ~70%, (2) moves through a manual JOURNAL VOUCHER / INTERNAL rail that bypasses normal AP/vendor-payment controls, and (3) sits in a discretionary procurement category. Three independent oversight enablers are missing at once. In the data, 100% of these rows also carry an 'N/A' or NULL payee, so they represent the most opaque slice. Fires on 8,016 rows / $5.34B = 2.61% of the >=$100K population -- a strict subset of the generic-payee marker (A_and_B = 8,016, B_not_A = 0) but elevated to severity 5 by the compounded gaps.
markers.guardrails: vid IS NULL is REQUIRED and fiscal_year NOT IN (2019,2020) is hard-coded, so the marker can NEVER fire merely because vid is system-wide absent in a thin year (the critical degrade-gracefully requirement). Manual-rail restricted to JOURNAL VOUCHER/INTERNAL (not ACH/WARRANT, which are normal vendor payments). Discretionary category filter excludes entitlement/scheduled spend. Because it is a subset of the generic_placeholder_payee marker, in a combined score it should be treated as an ESCALATOR of those rows rather than additive double-counting -- document the overlap so the two are not summed independently.

examples.agency: DEPT OF EMERGENCY AND MILITARY AFFAIRS
examples.why: $92M CAPITAL EQUIPMENT (border fence) -- vid NULL + INTERNAL rail + N/A payee: all three oversight enablers absent on a very large capital purchase.

$60M OTHER OPERATING ('COP DEBT SERVICE') via INTERNAL rail with NULL payee and no vid -- though labeled debt-service-adjacent, it is booked under a discretionary category with full triple gap; confirm classification and counterparty.
$35M CAPITAL OUTLAY freeway construction via JOURNAL VOUCHER, vid NULL, NULL payee -- large capital JV with no traceable vendor; high-priority item to confirm.

markers.name: Large procurement to a named real vendor with no contract reference
markers.sql_predicate: amount >= 1000000 AND fiscal_year NOT IN (2019,2020) AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','CAPITAL OUTLAY') AND contract_number IS NULL AND vid IS NOT NULL AND payee IS NOT NULL AND payee NOT IN ('N/A','NULL','MISCELLANEOUS VENDOR','MISCELLANEOUS CUSTOMER') AND upper(trim(payee)) <> upper(trim(agency))
markers.applicable_years: FY2016-2018, FY2021, FY2023-2025. Excludes FY2019-2020 (vid NULL system-wide; cannot satisfy vid IS NOT NULL there anyway). Note FY2019/2020 actually DO carry some contract_number values, but the vid gate makes the marker dark in those years by design to keep it a real gap.
markers.rationale: A $1M+ professional-services or capital-outlay payment to a real, identifiable vendor (vid present) that carries NO contract_number is a procurement-documentation gap: a million-dollar consulting or construction payment should trace to a contract instrument. Distinct from markers A/B (those have generic/no payee; this has a fully named vendor) -- A_and_C overlap = 0, so it adds genuinely new leads. Fires on 1,474 rows / $4.67B = 0.48% of the >=$100K population. Examples include $20-28M capital-outlay payments to P3 concessionaires (CONNECT 202 PARTNERS) and land-title agents (SECURITY TITLE AGENCY) with no contract reference.
markers.guardrails: MODERATE severity (2), NOT severe, because contract_number is only ~2-6% populated mart-wide -- absence may reflect a data-feed limitation rather than a truly missing contract, so this is explicitly an 'item to confirm,' never an assertion that no contract exists. vid IS NOT NULL is REQUIRED, which both ensures a real named vendor and makes the marker dark in thin years (never fires on a system-wide-absent field). Self-payments are excluded via upper(trim(payee)) <> upper(trim(agency)) -- this removed a benign-by-construction artifact where PSPRS paid ITSELF $99M for 'unfunded liability reduction' (an internal funding transfer, not a procurement, so no contract is expected). Generic payee tokens excluded so it does not double-count marker A. Category restricted to true procurement (PROFESSIONAL/CAPITAL OUTLAY); OTHER OPERATING was dropped because it mixed in non-procurement internal transfers.

examples.why: $28M CAPITAL OUTLAY to a named title agency (likely land acquisition) with vid present but no contract_number on the record -- confirm the underlying contract/escrow instrument.

$24M CAPITAL OUTLAY to a named P3 highway concessionaire with no contract reference -- a payment of this size to a design-build-finance partner should trace to a concession agreement; item to confirm.
$24M CAPITAL OUTLAY (Federal Aid) to the same concessionaire, again with no contract_number recorded -- recurring large procurement payments lacking the contract linkage that enables oversight.

specs.notes:
CALIBRATION & VALIDATION (all stats restricted to amount>=100000; population = 306,604; queries run against mart/hv_base.parquet only):
- Marker A fires 15,047 (4.91%, $8.92B); Marker B 8,016 (2.61%, $5.34B); Marker C 1,474 (0.48%, $4.67B). Union of all three = 16,521 rows (5.39%) -- complementary, not redundant.
- OVERLAP MAP (measured, not assumed): B is a STRICT SUBSET of A (A_and_B=8,016, B_not_A=0); B should be used as a severity ESCALATOR on A's rows, NOT summed additively, or the same dollars double-count. C is fully DISJOINT from A (A_and_C=0) -- different gap class (named vendor + missing contract vs. generic/absent payee), so C adds independent leads.

FIELDS I DISCARDED AS NON-DISCRIMINATING (important negative results):
- appropriation_1_code IS NULL: in all non-thin years it is 100% populated; only 1 row in good years is NULL. NULL apcode is purely a FY2019/2020 thin-year artifact, so I did NOT build a marker on it (it would only ever fire on thin years -- exactly the forbidden behavior).
- Bare 'missing contract' (without the $1M + named-vendor gates): contract_number is present on only ~37-46% of professional/capital items even in good years, so a standalone 'no contract' marker would fire on ~55-60% of discretionary procurement = pure noise. I tightened it to >=$1M + vid present + non-self-payment to reach 0.48%.
- NULL vid alone in good years: 70,771 rows = 28.7% of pop -- far too broad (vid is only ~70% populated even in good years). I made it discriminating only in combination (Marker B = NULL vid + manual rail + discretionary).

FEATURES NEEDED BUT ABSENT FROM THE MART:
- payee_dba_name 'differs oddly from payee' sub-idea is NOT IMPLEMENTABLE: payee_dba_name is 100% NULL in the >=$100K population (system-wide absent), so any predicate over it would be dead. To enable it, the mart would need a populated payee_dba_name plus a derived similarity feature, e.g. dba_payee_token_jaccard DOUBLE (token-set Jaccard between payee and payee_dba_name) and a boolean dba_present_and_divergent (payee_dba_name IS NOT NULL AND jaccard < 0.3) -- compute with WORD/TOKEN matching, never substring, to avoid the ZTE/Aztec class of false positive.
- A clean 'contract expected' flag would sharpen Marker C: a boolean procurement_contract_expected derived from object_no / appropriation_category / contract-vehicle metadata would let us distinguish 'contract genuinely missing' from 'contract not expected for this spend type' and could raise C's severity above 2.

ZTE/AZTEC GUARDRAIL APPLIED THROUGHOUT: every payee match is exact-equality or explicit-word-boundary regex '(^| )(PCARD|FUEL) VENDOR( |$)'; I verified that a naive substring LIKE '%TEMP%' spuriously matched CITY OF TEMPE, TEMPLE EMANUEL, CONTEMPORARY SERVICES, TEMPEST INC, etc., and avoided all substring matching accordingly.

CATEGORY RISK-WEIGHTING: all three markers are confined to DISCRETIONARY procurement categories (PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES, OTHER OPERATING, CAPITAL OUTLAY/EQUIPMENT, NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT) and deliberately EXCLUDE the scheduled/entitlement categories (AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS, TRANSFERS OUT, DEBT SERVICE, PERSONAL SERVICES) where missing payee/contract detail is normal-by-construction (privacy-redacted benefit recipients, payroll, debt schedules).

FRAMING: every marker yields 'items to confirm' / leads warranting review; none names a wrongdoer or asserts fraud. Marker C in particular is severity 2 specifically because contract_number sparsity means its absence may be a data-feed gap, not a true missing contract.

specs.family: Discretionary-procurement concentration (large, off-contract, vendor-concentrated procurement lacking competitive-bid linkage)

markers.name: Large off-contract discretionary procurement (moderate tier)
markers.sql_predicate: amount >= 100000 AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT') AND contract_number IS NULL AND vid IS NOT NULL AND coalesce(category2,'') <> 'INTRASTATE DISTRIBUTIONS' AND payee IS DISTINCT FROM agency
markers.applicable_years: FY2016, FY2017, FY2018, FY2021, FY2023, FY2024, FY2025. NOT FY2019/FY2020 (vid is NULL system-wide in those years, so the vid IS NOT NULL guardrail correctly suppresses the marker rather than firing blindly). FY2022 is absent from the mart entirely.
markers.rationale: Isolates the highest competitive-bidding-risk surface: large (>=$100K) spend in the three true discretionary-procurement category1 families (PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES, OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES, CAPITAL OUTLAY/EQUIPMENT) that carries NO contract_number linkage. This mirrors the AG 'vendors without fair/open competition' finding: a real third-party vendor (vid present) receiving a material payment with no visible contract is a lead warranting review of whether a competitive solicitation existed. Entitlement/scheduled families (AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS, TRANSFERS OUT, DEBT SERVICE, COST ALLOCATION, PERSONAL/EMPLOYEE-RELATED) are deliberately excluded because large amounts there are normal and not procurement. This is a 'leads warranting review' screen, not a finding of fraud; nothing here names a wrongdoer.
markers.guardrails: (1) vid IS NOT NULL requires a REAL vendor id, which both proves the payee is an external vendor (not a sentinel/intra-state shell) AND auto-suppresses the marker in thin years FY2019/FY2020 where vid is NULL system-wide -- so we never penalize a transaction for a field that is absent by year. (2) coalesce(category2,'')<>'INTRASTATE DISTRIBUTIONS' removes benign intra-governmental distributions miscoded under OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES (e.g. PSPRS pension unfunded-liability transfers). (3) payee IS DISTINCT FROM agency removes self-payee internal transfers (NULL-safe). (4) Category whitelist (not a substring match) avoids the ZTE/Aztec class of error -- we match exact category1 strings, never substrings of names. (5) contract_number is only ~8-30% populated even in good years, so absence is the screening signal, NOT proof of off-contract spend; an absent linkage in this field can mean the contract exists but was not keyed -- hence moderate severity and 'item to confirm', not accusation. Known residual: ADOT CAPITAL OUTLAY construction frequently runs on engineering/construction contracts whose number is simply not linked in this field; reviewers should de-prioritize ADOT FEDERAL AID / M-RARF construction draws.

examples.why: $10.2M to a single health-benefits administrator under NON APPROPRIATED BENEFITS PROGRAM with no contract_number linked and the vendor holding 25% of the agency's category spend that year -- confirm the underlying benefits-admin contract and solicitation.
$12.5M off-contract to an LLC under WATER BANKING AUTHORITY, 35% of agency category spend -- large concentrated professional-services payment warranting confirmation of competitive procurement.
Tax-system vendor paid off-contract under REVENUE COLLECTIONS -- confirm the IT contract vehicle backing the payment.
Professional-services payment with no contract linkage to a major outsourcing vendor -- verify solicitation.

markers.name: Off-contract discretionary procurement, large AND vendor-concentrated (severe tier)
markers.sql_predicate: amount >= 1000000 AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT') AND contract_number IS NULL AND vid IS NOT NULL AND coalesce(category2,'') <> 'INTRASTATE DISTRIBUTIONS' AND payee IS DISTINCT FROM agency AND vendor_share_of_agency_year >= 0.20
markers.applicable_years: FY2016, FY2017, FY2018, FY2021, FY2023, FY2024, FY2025 (vid IS NOT NULL excludes thin FY2019/FY2020; FY2022 not in mart).
markers.rationale: Adds two discriminating dimensions on top of the base: (a) amount >= $1M (material single payment) and (b) vendor_share_of_agency_year >= 20% (the vendor captures a dominant slice of the agency's spend that year -- a concentration signal consistent with sole-source / no-open-competition risk). The combination of off-contract + large + concentrated is the strongest standalone competitive-bidding red flag in the procurement families. Still a review lead, not a fraud finding.
markers.guardrails: Inherits all base guardrails (real-vid external-vendor gate that also kills thin years, INTRASTATE DISTRIBUTIONS exclusion, NULL-safe self-payee exclusion, exact category whitelist not substring). The INTRASTATE/self-payee guardrails are LOAD-BEARING here: without them this tier was dominated by PSPRS pension-fund self-distributions ($99M intra-governmental transfers, 99% share) -- exactly the benign-by-construction artifact to suppress; guardrails cut the tier from 1,835 to 588 rows. vendor_share_of_agency_year is a clean double in the mart (no thin-year NULL issue). Residual caveat: LOTTERY COMMISSION 'RESALE SUPPLIES' to Bank of America (prize/payment-processing mechanics) appears here and is operationally explainable -- keep but de-prioritize; confirm it is a banking-services contract vehicle.

examples.why: $10.2M, 25% of agency category spend, no contract -- dominant single benefits vendor warranting solicitation confirmation.
$12.5M single LLC payment, 35% concentration, off-contract -- high competitive-bidding-review priority.
Concentrated off-contract construction-in-progress spend at a smaller agency where one vendor dominates the year -- confirm procurement method.
Large professional-services payment, no contract, dominant vendor share at a small office -- review solicitation.

markers.name: Off-contract concentrated procurement that is also a peer-group statistical outlier (very-severe tier)
markers.sql_predicate: amount >= 1000000 AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT') AND contract_number IS NULL AND vid IS NOT NULL AND coalesce(category2,'') <> 'INTRASTATE DISTRIBUTIONS' AND payee IS DISTINCT FROM agency AND vendor_share_of_agency_year >= 0.20 AND robust_z_in_agencycat >= 10
markers.applicable_years: FY2016, FY2017, FY2018, FY2021, FY2023, FY2024, FY2025 (thin years excluded via vid; FY2022 not in mart).
markers.rationale: Strongest standalone signal: every condition of the severe tier PLUS robust_z_in_agencycat >= 10, meaning the payment is a >=10-IQR outlier versus the median transaction in its own agency x category1 peer group. This selects off-contract, concentrated payments that are ALSO anomalously large relative to peers -- the narrowest, highest-confidence lead set for manual review.
markers.guardrails: All prior guardrails apply. robust_z_in_agencycat is computed within agency x category1 so it is self-normalizing (a $1M payment in a small agency scores high; the same in a big procurement shop may not), which is the intended behavior. robust_z is a double present in all retained years (not thin-year-gated), but the vid gate already removes FY2019/2020. Because this tier is small (274 rows), treat it as a curated review queue, not an automated action; each is an 'item to confirm.'

examples.why: Off-contract, 25% vendor concentration, AND a >10-IQR outlier vs other DOA other-operating transactions -- top-priority confirm of contract/solicitation.
Single $12.5M LLC payment that is a strong peer-group outlier at a small agency, off-contract -- highest review weight.
$7M off-contract concentrated payment that dwarfs peer transactions in the agency category -- confirm procurement vehicle.

specs.notes:
CALIBRATION: All three predicates were run against mart/hv_base.parquet. Headline stats restricted to amount>=100000 and reported against the 306,604-row >=$100K population. Moderate base = 18,685 rows (6.09%, $16.6B) -> proper 'moderate' band (<15%). Severe = 588 rows (0.19%, $2.07B) and very-severe = 274 rows (0.09%, $1.61B) -> both well under the 5% 'severe' ceiling. The three markers are NESTED (very-severe subset of severe subset of base), so they should compose as severity TIERS, not be summed.

KEY GUARDRAIL FINDING (false-positive class avoided): the un-guarded severe predicate was dominated by PUBLIC SAFETY PERSONNEL RETIREMENT SYSTEM paying ITSELF $99M (category2='INTRASTATE DISTRIBUTIONS', 99% share) -- scheduled intra-governmental pension unfunded-liability transfers miscoded under OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES. These are benign-by-construction, exactly the ZTE/Aztec class of error in spirit. Two guardrails neutralize them: coalesce(category2,'')<>'INTRASTATE DISTRIBUTIONS' and payee IS DISTINCT FROM agency. Together they removed ~1,247 false positives from the severe tier (1,835 -> 588). The vid IS NOT NULL gate does double duty: it proves an external vendor AND mechanically excludes FY2019/FY2020 where vid is NULL system-wide, so the markers degrade gracefully (suppress) rather than mis-fire in thin years -- verified: zero FY2019/FY2020 rows survive.

NAME MATCHING: no substring/name matching is used anywhere; all category gating is exact-string equality on category1/category2, immune to the Aztec/ZTE substring problem.

OVERLAP WITH OTHER FAMILIES: (a) vendor_first_appearance overlaps a 'new-vendor' family -- ~4,951 base rows are first-appearance; consider that family for vendors brand-new to the state rather than double-counting here. (b) vendor_share>=50% overlaps a 'vendor-dominance/single-source' family. (c) robust_z>=10 overlaps a generic 'statistical-outlier' family (14.4% baseline) -- I use it only as the top tier's amplifier, not standalone, to avoid noise. (d) The Lottery/Bank-of-America prize/resale cluster (category2 RESALE SUPPLIES) and ADOT FEDERAL AID/M-RARF construction draws are the two most explainable residuals reviewers will see; both are operationally plausible (payment-processing mechanics; construction on contracts not keyed to contract_number) and should be de-prioritized but not auto-excluded.

FEATURES NEEDED BUT ABSENT FROM MART: (1) A reliable contract-coverage flag -- contract_number is only ~8-30% populated even in good years, so its absence is a weak proxy for 'truly off-contract'; a 'vendor-has-ANY-contract-with-this-agency-this-year' boolean would sharpen this materially. Placeholder name: vendor_agency_has_contract_year. (2) A solicitation/procurement-method code (sole-source vs competitive) would let us target the AG finding directly; not in mart. (3) A self-payee / intra-governmental flag would be cleaner than the agency=payee + INTRASTATE heuristic; placeholder: is_intragovernmental. (4) object_no (procurement object detail) is FY2016-2018 only, so not usable as a cross-year guardrail. All three returned predicates use only existing hv_base columns and were executed successfully; no placeholder columns appear in the predicates themselves.

markers.name: Individual-person-style payee in discretionary/non-benefit category (possible related party)
markers.sql_predicate: amount >= 100000 AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','EMPLOYEE RELATED EXPENDITURES') AND payee ~ '^[A-Z][A-Z''-]+ [A-Z][A-Z''-]+$' AND NOT regexp_matches(payee, '(INC|LLC|LLP|CORP|COMPANY|LTD|PLLC|ASSOC|GROUP|TRUST|BANK|COUNTY|UNIV|DEPT|SERVICE|SYSTEM|SOLUTION|TECH|CONSULT|ENTERPRISE|HOLDING|PARTNER|FUND|FOUNDATION|HOSPITAL|CLINIC|CENTER|CENTRE|INSTITUTE|SCHOOL|DISTRICT|HEALTH|MEDICAL|INSURANCE|CONSTRUCT|ELECTRIC|SUPPLY|EQUIP|PRODUCT|INDUSTR|MGMT|MANAGEMENT|CAPITAL|FINANCIAL|REALTY|PROPERT|MOTOR|AUTO|ENERGY|POWER|WATER|COMMUNICAT|MEDIA|PRESS|ENGINEER|ARCHITECT|PHARM|NETWORK|SOFTWARE|DESIGN|CONTRACT|BUILDER|FARM|RANCH|MARKET|STORE|FOOD|INTERNATIONAL|NATIONAL|GLOBAL|AMERICA|DIGITAL|CREATIVE|DEVELOP|LEARNING|DISTRIBUT|NEXTEL|INSIGHT|VENDOR|CUSTOMER|MISC|VARIOUS|REDACTED|TRANSPORT|MINISTR|CASINO|RISK|LENS|PIPELINE|STORAGE|TRAURIG|ROCK|CHEVROLET|KENWORTH)')
markers.applicable_years: FY2016-2025 (all years incl. thin FY2019/2020; FY2022 absent from mart). Marker depends only on payee string + category1, NOT on vid/appr_type/fp_canon, so it runs and is valid in every available year.
markers.rationale: A payee that parses as a two-token all-caps personal name (FIRSTNAME LASTNAME) receiving a >=$100K discretionary-procurement disbursement is a related-party / direct-to-individual lead. All payees in this mart are uppercase and corporate names dominate, so a clean two-token alpha string with no business-entity keyword is a strong structural signal of a natural person rather than a firm. Real examples (TALONYA ADAMS $300K Risk Management, KENNETH PORTER $398K Corrections AOOE, JOSE SORTO $604K ADOT freeway construction, JOANNE BOALER $167K Governor ARPA, JAMES ADAMS $283K Land Dept broker commissions) are exactly the items an auditor would confirm: many are legitimate (legal settlements/judgments paid to named plaintiffs, expert witnesses, individual brokers) but each warrants a confirm because individuals receiving six-figure discretionary procurement money is where related-party/ghost-vendor risk concentrates. Calibrated to 0.069% of the >=$100K population (211 rows / 54 distinct names), well inside the 'severe' rule-of-thumb band, so it is discriminating not noise.
markers.guardrails: (1) WORD-BOUNDARY/regexp_matches business-keyword exclusion (NOT substring) directly answers the ZTE/Aztec failure mode: company names containing INC/LLC/CORP/COUNTY/HEALTH/etc. are excluded so 'PITNEY BOWES'/'RR DONNELLEY'/'GRANITE CONSTRUCTION' do not fire. (2) Scoped to DISCRETIONARY categories only; entitlement/benefit categories (AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS, TRANSFERS OUT, DEBT SERVICE) and PERSONAL SERVICES payroll are excluded, so privacy-redacted benefit recipients are never flagged. (3) Excludes 'NAME REDACTED'/'N/A' sentinels via the keyword list. (4) SEVERITY HELD AT 3 (not 5) BY DESIGN: legitimate sole proprietors, individual contractors, expert witnesses, and litigation-settlement payees with common surnames exist; this is a lead-to-confirm, never an accusation. (5) CONFIRMATION STEP (recommended, requires a feature not in mart): validate split_part(payee,' ',1) against a US Census/SSA given-name gazetteer (a needs_firstname_gazetteer flag) to raise precision and drop two-token brand names like 'CUSTOM STORAGE'/'ORIGAMI RISK' that survive the keyword filter. With a gazetteer the fire set tightens further (~23 rows on a tiny demo list), so the structural predicate here is the recall-side net and the gazetteer is the precision-side confirm. (6) Independent of vid/appr_type, so it never penalizes a thin-year (FY19/20) row for a system-wide-absent field.

examples.why: Individual-person payee receiving $604.5K under M-RARF freeway-construction appropriation (CAPITAL OUTLAY); a natural-person name on a six-figure construction disbursement is a related-party/direct-payment item to confirm (e.g. right-of-way land purchase vs. individual contractor).

Same individual-name payee appears repeatedly (FY23-24) totaling ~$877K across NON-HEALTH CARE AOOE and CAPITAL OUTLAY APPN; recurring six-figure payments to a named individual in discretionary operating/capital lines warrant vendor-identity confirmation.

Two identical $300K payments under RISK MANAGEMENT LOSSES AND PREMIUMS to a named individual; likely a legitimate litigation settlement but the duplicate-amount + individual-payee combination is a confirm-worthy lead (settlement docs / no-related-party attestation).

$283K to an individual under BROKER COMMISSIONS; individual broker payments are legitimate by nature but six-figure commissions to a natural person are exactly the population to confirm against the broker registry.

$167K to a named individual from the CORONAVIRUS STATE & LOCAL FISCAL RECOVERY FUND (ARPA) appropriation; individual payee on federal-recovery discretionary grant money is a heightened-scrutiny confirm item.

markers.name: Generic/placeholder/masked payee on an EXTERNAL payment rail in discretionary procurement
markers.sql_predicate: amount >= 100000 AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT') AND payment_method IN ('ACH','WARRANT','PAYMENT') AND (payee IS NULL OR upper(payee) IN ('N/A','NA','NONE','NULL','UNKNOWN','TBD','VOID') OR regexp_matches(payee, '(^|\s)(MISC|MISCELLANEOUS|VARIOUS|VENDOR|CUSTOMER|PAYEE|PLACEHOLDER|TEMP|TEMPORARY|TEST|DUMMY|DEFAULT|SUNDRY|PCARD|FUEL VENDOR)(\s|$)'))
markers.applicable_years: FY2016-2025 (all years incl. thin FY2019/2020; FY2022 absent). Depends only on payee + payment_method + category1; none are NULL in thin years for the external-rail subset, so it runs cleanly every year.
markers.rationale: An external disbursement (ACH/WARRANT/PAYMENT — money actually leaving the state to a third party) of >=$100K in a discretionary procurement category where the payee is a placeholder ('N/A', NULL) or a deliberately masked aggregate ('MISCELLANEOUS VENDOR', 'MISCELLANEOUS CUSTOMER', 'PCARD VENDOR PSA', 'FUEL VENDOR PSA') means a material sum left the door with no identified beneficiary. Examples: STATE TREASURER $2.23M WARRANT to 'MISCELLANEOUS VENDOR' (Fund Administration), DEPT OF CORRECTIONS two $750K WARRANTs to 'MISCELLANEOUS VENDOR' (Non-Health-Care AOOE), LAND DEPARTMENT multiple $300-500K WARRANTs to 'MISCELLANEOUS CUSTOMER' (Broker Commissions). These are review-worthy because an unidentified payee defeats vendor-master controls and is the canonical place ghost/fictitious-vendor or split-disbursement schemes hide. Calibrated to 0.055% of the >=$100K population (169 rows, $27.3M) — squarely 'severe'-tier discriminating.
markers.guardrails: (1) CRITICAL RAIL GUARDRAIL: restrict to external rails ('ACH','WARRANT','PAYMENT') and EXCLUDE internal-accounting rails ('INTERNAL','JOURNAL VOUCHER','COST ALLOCATION'). Without this the marker balloons to ~14,900 rows / $8.9B because 'N/A' is the normal, benign placeholder on internal cost-allocation and inter-fund transfer entries (no external payee exists by design). The rail filter is what converts a benign-by-construction artifact into a real lead. (2) Scoped to discretionary procurement categories only; entitlement/transfer/debt-service categories where placeholders are routine are excluded. (3) WORD-BOUNDARY regex ((^|\s)...(\s|$)) so 'VENDOR'/'CUSTOMER' match only as standalone tokens and never as a substring inside a legitimate firm name (e.g. avoids flagging a real company whose name contains 'VENDOR'). (4) Independent of vid/appr_type, so no thin-year penalty. (5) Severity 3, not 5: many of these are legitimate pooled/p-card or escrow-style aggregations and broker-commission clearing accounts — each is an item to confirm (who was actually paid?), not a finding.

examples.why: $2.23M WARRANT (external rail) under FUND ADMINISTRATION with no identified payee; a multi-million-dollar disbursement to a masked aggregate defeats the vendor master and is a top confirm item (who received the funds?).
Two separate $750K WARRANTs to 'MISCELLANEOUS VENDOR' under NON-HEALTH CARE AOOE; round-dollar six-figure payments to an unidentified payee on an external rail warrant supporting-document review.

$500K WARRANT under BROKER COMMISSIONS to a masked 'CUSTOMER' placeholder; recurring $300-500K masked-payee broker-commission warrants are a confirm cluster (identify the actual commission recipients).

$294.9K ACH to a p-card aggregation placeholder under DPS JOINT FUND; a single $295K p-card-pool ACH should be decomposed to confirm the underlying merchants and that no single transaction exceeds card limits.
$325K WARRANT to unidentified vendor under DDD/LTC OPERATING CLR; an unnamed payee on a quarter-million external disbursement in a discretionary operating line is a clean lead-to-confirm.

specs.notes:
OVERLAPS WITH OTHER FAMILIES: (a) The placeholder-payee marker correlates with the 'manual/unusual rail' family only at its boundary — by design I REQUIRE external rails and EXCLUDE the manual internal rails (JV/INTERNAL/COST ALLOCATION), so it is complementary to, not duplicative of, a manual-rail marker. (b) Both markers will co-occur with round-dollar markers (several examples are exact $300K/$500K/$750K); when composing the overall score, treat round-dollar as an amplifier on these payee anomalies rather than double-counting. (c) The person-name marker can overlap a 'vendor_first_appearance' / single-txn-vendor marker (most named individuals are one-shot payees) — that combination should RAISE confidence, not be deduped away.

FEATURES NEEDED BUT ABSENT FROM THE MART:
1) payee_dba_name IS 100% NULL system-wide (0 of 1,015,977 rows populated). The requested 'DBA != legal name' sub-marker is therefore UNRUNNABLE on this mart. Placeholder predicate if the field is ever backfilled: `payee_dba_name IS NOT NULL AND upper(regexp_replace(payee_dba_name,'[^A-Z0-9]','','g')) <> upper(regexp_replace(payee,'[^A-Z0-9]','','g'))` — fires when the doing-business-as name normalizes differently from the legal payee (classic shell/pass-through signal). Add payee_dba_name to the mart to enable.
2) FIRST-NAME GAZETTEER: the person-name marker's precision is capped because I cannot ship a multi-thousand-name given-name dictionary inline as a SQL predicate. Recommend adding a boolean column `payee_token1_is_given_name` = (split_part(payee,' ',1) is in a US SSA/Census given-name list). Predicate would then become the structural pattern AND payee_token1_is_given_name, raising precision and dropping two-token brand names ('CUSTOM STORAGE','ORIGAMI RISK','WW CLYDE') that currently survive the keyword blocklist. The inline keyword blocklist in the shipped predicate is an interim approximation tuned on observed false positives and WILL need maintenance; the gazetteer column is the durable fix.
3) NEAR-DUPLICATE VENDOR-NAME VARIANTS (the 'shell-company variant' sub-family): I prototyped it (normalize payee to an alnum key stripping INC/LLC/CORP suffixes, group by key, flag keys with >=3 distinct raw forms). RESULT: it is NOT recommended as a standalone marker on this mart. The collisions are almost entirely benign punctuation/escaping/suffix data-entry noise on LEGITIMATE large vendors — e.g. 'CENPATICO OF ARIZONA, INC.' vs 'CENPATICO OF ARIZONA\\, INC' vs 'CENPATICO OF ARIZONA LLC' ($1.85B, a real Medicaid MCO), 'WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.' variants, 'GRANITE CONSTRUCTION CO/COMPANY'. Flagging these would reproduce the false-positive class the engagement warns against (benign-by-construction). It also cannot be expressed as a single-row boolean predicate (requires a self-join/window over the population), so it does not fit the compose-into-one-query constraint. If pursued, it belongs in a separate vendor-master-dedup pass with a clean normalized vendor key, NOT in this row-level marker set. The raw '\\,' escaped-comma artifacts also indicate an upstream ingestion-quality issue worth fixing before any name-similarity work.

CALIBRATION REASONING: Headline stats restricted to amount>=100000 against the 306,604-row >=$100K population per instructions. Person-name = 211 rows / 0.069%; placeholder-external-rail = 169 rows / 0.055%. Both sit far below the 5% 'severe' ceiling, confirming they are discriminating leads rather than population-level noise. I held both at severity 3 (not 5) because each family contains a real benign sub-population (legitimate sole proprietors / litigation-settlement individuals; legitimate pooled p-card and broker-commission clearing accounts) — these are confirm-worthy leads, never findings of fraud, and nothing here names a wrongdoer. Both markers verified stable across all fiscal years including thin FY2019/2020 (they depend on no field that is system-wide NULL in those years), so no graceful-degradation gating is required.

markers.name: Agency-level Benford digit nonconformity (corroborated first + second digit)
markers.sql_predicate: agency_benford_flag = TRUE AND amount >= 100000 AND category1 NOT IN ('AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS','TRANSFERS OUT','DEBT SERVICE')
markers.applicable_years: FY2016-2018, FY2021, FY2023-2025 AND FY2019-2020. The agency_benford_flag depends ONLY on agency, amount, and first_digit, all of which are fully populated (0 NULLs) in every year including the thin FY2019/FY2020. It does NOT use vid, appr_type_canon, or fp_canon, so it degrades gracefully and runs in thin years. FY2022 is absent from the mart entirely. MAD is computed pooled across ALL available years per agency to maximize sample size (a population diagnostic, not a per-year test).
markers.rationale: Within the >=$100K population, each agency's leading-digit and second-digit frequency distribution is compared to the Benford expectation via Mean Absolute Deviation (MAD). An agency is flagged only when BOTH its first-digit MAD AND its second-digit MAD fall in the upper quartile (>p75) of the agency peer distribution, with a minimum sample of n>=500 high-value txns. Requiring corroboration across two independent digit tests is the core anti-false-positive control: it rejects agencies that deviate on only one test (e.g. NORTHERN ARIZONA UNIVERSITY has high second-digit MAD 0.0757 but only moderate first-digit MAD 0.0460, so it is correctly NOT flagged). Absolute Nigrini thresholds (MAD>0.015 = nonconformity) are NOT used because a population truncated at $100K is inherently not full-range Benford data — every one of the 34 qualifying agencies exceeds 0.015, so the absolute test has zero discrimination here. A relative (peer-percentile) test is the correct stratum design. The flag elevates an agency's discretionary transactions modestly; it is a directional 'where to look first' signal that estimated/rounded/fabricated amounts may be over-represented, never a per-transaction accusation. 5 agencies are flagged: SCHOOLS FOR THE DEAF AND THE BLIND (mad1 0.1148/mad2 0.0477), CORPORATION COMMISSION (0.1097/0.0568), DEPT OF VETERANS SERVICES (0.1065/0.0503), ATTORNEY GENERAL (0.0960/0.0336), DEPT OF PUBLIC SAFETY (0.0902/0.0422).
markers.guardrails: (1) ENTITLEMENT EXCLUSION: even within a flagged agency, scheduled/entitlement categories (AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS, TRANSFERS OUT, DEBT SERVICE) are excluded — large scheduled amounts are expected and Benford conformity is not a fraud signal there; this drops exposure from $5.24B to $1.97B by removing $3.0B of TRANSFERS OUT. (2) CORROBORATION: requires BOTH first- AND second-digit MAD above peer p75, so a single-test outlier cannot trigger the flag (prevents the NAU-type false positive). (3) MINIMUM SAMPLE: n>=500 high-value txns per agency, so digit frequencies are statistically meaningful and small agencies cannot be flagged on noise. (4) NO NAME MATCHING: this marker keys on agency name as an exact categorical grouping, not substring — so it is immune to the 'Aztec'->'ZTE' substring class of error. (5) THIN-YEAR SAFE: uses no field that is NULL in FY2019/2020. (6) LOW WEIGHT: severity 2, never standalone — it elevates an agency's transactions modestly and should only escalate when combined with a transaction-level signal (round-amount, vendor-concentration, duplicate). (7) PAYROLL CAVEAT (note, not hard-coded): PERSONAL SERVICES is the largest discretionary category in the flagged set ($882M); payroll amounts follow salary schedules, so reviewers should down-weight digit signals on PERSONAL SERVICES / EMPLOYEE RELATED EXPENDITURES and focus on PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES, OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES, CAPITAL OUTLAY/EQUIPMENT (procurement-only subset = 2,763 txns / $846.6M).

examples.why: Round $750K under 'LAND MOBILE RADIO EXPANSION AND UPGRADES' (OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES) at a flagged agency. A legitimate-looking procurement, but the round amount plus the agency's digit nonconformity makes it a confirm-the-basis lead, not an accusation.

Round $650K against 'OPERATING LUMP SUM APPROPRIATION' (OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES) with no named payee. Lump-sum + round + N/A payee at a digit-nonconformant agency is the estimated/fabricated-amount pattern this family targets; review the supporting detail.

Round $900K CAPITAL EQUIPMENT with NULL appropriation and no named payee at a flagged agency (round_100k = true). Item to confirm: what asset and which vendor underlie an exactly-$900K capital outlay.

$520K (round-10k) OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES, NULL appropriation, at a flagged agency; same payee also appears at exactly $500K. Repeated round amounts to one payee in a digit-nonconformant agency warrant a look at how the amounts were set.

examples.agency: SCHOOLS FOR THE DEAF AND THE BLIND
examples.why: Exactly $500K CAPITAL OUTLAY, NULL appropriation, no named payee, at the agency with the highest first-digit MAD (0.1148). Confirm the underlying capital project and vendor for this exactly-round capital amount.

specs.notes:
PREDICATE / PLACEHOLDER COLUMN: agency_benford_flag is NOT in the mart and must be added. It is a boolean computed once per agency over the >=$100K population, then joined back to every row by agency. Precise definition: (a) restrict to amount>=100000; (b) FIRST-DIGIT MAD per agency = mean over digits 1-9 of |observed_freq - benford_p1| where benford_p1 = log10(1+1/d) (0.30103,0.17609,0.12494,0.09691,0.07918,0.06695,0.05799,0.05115,0.04576); (c) SECOND-DIGIT MAD per agency = mean over digits 0-9 of |observed_freq - benford_p2| where benford_p2 = (0.11968,0.11389,0.10882,0.10433,0.10031,0.09668,0.09337,0.09035,0.08757,0.08500), second digit taken as substr of the integer-dollar string position 2; (d) keep agencies with n>=500; (e) compute p75 of mad1 and p75 of mad2 across those agencies; (f) agency_benford_flag = (mad1 > p75(mad1) AND mad2 > p75(mad2)). With current data this resolves to exactly 5 agencies: SCHOOLS FOR THE DEAF AND THE BLIND, CORPORATION COMMISSION, DEPT OF VETERANS SERVICES, ATTORNEY GENERAL (DEPT OF LAW), DEPT OF PUBLIC SAFETY. The exact SQL was run against mart/hv_base.parquet and verified; an equivalent fully-inlined predicate is `agency IN ('SCHOOLS FOR THE DEAF AND THE BLIND','CORPORATION COMMISSION','DEPT OF VETERANS SERVICES','ATTORNEY GENERAL (DEPT OF LAW)','DEPT OF PUBLIC SAFETY')` if a precomputed column is not desired, but recompute the flag whenever new fiscal years load.

CALIBRATION: 34 agencies clear the n>=500 floor; median first-digit MAD 0.0565, p90 0.0960. The corroborated p75-on-both rule selects 5 agencies. In the >=$100K population these 5 hold 11,319 txns / $5.24B total; after the entitlement exclusion the marker fires on 9,070 txns / $1,968.1M = 3.0% of the 306,604-row >=$100K population (within the <5% 'severe' band, but weighted 2 because it is an agency-level diagnostic, not a transaction-specific signal). Procurement-only subset (PROF/OUTSIDE SVCS, OTHER OPERATING, CAPITAL OUTLAY/EQUIP) = 2,763 txns / $846.6M.

OVERLAPS: This family deliberately co-fires with (1) round-amount markers (is_round_100k/is_round_1m) — digit-nonconformity is the population-level explanation for which agencies the round-amount markers cluster in, so combining them is corroborative, not double-counting; weight the COMBINATION higher than either alone. (2) vendor-concentration markers (vendor_share_of_agency_year) — a single dominant vendor can itself bend an agency's digit distribution. (3) robust_z peer-outlier markers within agency x category1. Recommended composition: use agency_benford_flag as a multiplier/elevator on transaction-level markers within the agency, NOT as a standalone lead generator.

FEATURES THAT WOULD STRENGTHEN THIS BUT ARE ABSENT FROM THE MART: (1) a chi-square or Nigrini-Z statistic and p-value per agency (currently only MAD is derivable); (2) a per-agency 'excess repeated-digit' count (e.g. share of amounts whose digits are all identical or of form ABABAB) — repeated-digit excess is a classic fabrication tell but requires a dedicated computed column; (3) per-agency-PER-YEAR digit MAD to detect a single anomalous year within an otherwise-conformant agency (pooling across years can mask a one-year spike). All three should be added as precomputed agency(/year)-level columns and joined by agency.

DATA-QUALITY HONESTY: first_digit and amount have 0 NULLs in all 9 present fiscal years, so the flag computes identically in thin years FY2019/2020; no thin-year suppression is needed and none is applied. The marker uses no NULL-in-thin-year field. No substring name matching is used anywhere (agency is an exact categorical key), so the 'Aztec'->'ZTE' false-positive class cannot occur here.

marker_weights.reason: NOT an additive marker — it is the materiality BASE WEIGHT, not part of marker_sum. Used only as the mag_band rank that selects the row's magnitude band in the Tier matrix (M1=4.0..M6=0.5 as documented but the BAND, not the points, drives tiering). Excluded from the weighted marker sum to prevent a large amount alone manufacturing a risk flag.

NOT additive — it is the MULTIPLIER applied to marker_sum (range 0.3-1.5 per category1). risk_score = marker_sum * category_risk_multiplier. This is what suppresses entitlement (the $4.66B TRANSFERS OUT scores 0.0; a $318M AID payment firing 3 marker points scores only 1.2). Verified live.

Folded into the Tier MATRIX (magnitude x risk), not the marker sum. '>=$10M discretionary' is exactly M1-M3 x discretionary-category, which the matrix already captures via mag_band rank + category multiplier. Adding it as a separate point would double-count magnitude, which already drives the tier axis.

Same as severe — subsumed by the magnitude axis of the Tier matrix. '>=$1M discretionary' = M1-M4 x discretionary; counting it as marker points would re-introduce dollar-driven inflation the multiplier design explicitly avoids.

Round family contributes ONE value = the max tier that fires (markers are disjoint by construction: round_1m, round_100k-not-1m, whole-not-10k). Severity 5 faithful to spec; arms-length + ext-rail + discretionary guardrails intact. 195 disc hits.
Disjoint middle tier of the round family (NOT is_round_1m). Severity 4 per spec. Contributes only when the $1M tier did not fire. 1,087 disc hits.
Broadest disjoint round tier (NOT is_round_10k). Severity 3 per spec. Round family takes GREATEST of the three so a single estimate never scores 5+4+3.
Duplicate family contributes its MAX tier only. Different-day exact-dup to a procurement vendor is the strongest dup lead; severity 5 retained. Rare (7 rows) so no over-firing.
Same-day external dup, severity 3, mutually exclusive with diffday by construction. 494 rows.
Broad coverage net, severity 2. Strict superset of dup_diffday_discretionary, so the family GREATEST collapse means an entitlement diffday scores 2 and a discretionary diffday scores 5 — never both.
Concentration family MAX tier. >=20% external discretionary share, severity 4 per spec. Bank/pension/self-pay word-boundary suppression intact (BANK OF AMERICA matches, BANNER HEALTH does not).
Lower-severity dependency companion (>=33% any non-entitlement, banks retained as exposure). Severity 2. Family takes GREATEST(sole_source=4, dependency=2) so a row firing both scores 4, not 6.

NOT RUNNABLE on current mart (requires vendor_approp_year_share at the appropriation_1_code x year x vid grain, which the mart does not expose). Calibrated count 0. Carry as a future feature; contributes nothing today so it cannot inflate scores.

New-vendor family MAX tier, severity 5. fiscal_year>2016 (drops FY2016 left-censoring); FY2021 contribution HALVED (floor(w/2)) to neutralize the post-NULL-vid-gap re-appearance artifact (3,893 FY21 rows down-weighted). vid IS NOT NULL keeps it dark in FY2019/2020.
Broad new-vendor net, severity 3. Superset of the dominant-share marker; family GREATEST collapse means a dominant new vendor scores 5 (not 5+3). Same FY2021 halving and FY2016 exclusion.

Short-tenure (<=2yr active) >=$2M JV/SPV catcher, severity 4. Orthogonal to first-appearance (catches prior-year-first JVs like Coffman Ames); family takes GREATEST so it does not stack additively with the first-appearance tiers.
Year-end family MAX tier. Broad period-13 book-entry flag, severity 3. fp_canon-gated so it is dark in FY2019/2020 (fp_canon NULL) — graceful degradation, never penalizes the thin years.
Sharpened period-13 (discretionary only), severity 4. Family GREATEST(p13_all=3, p13_disc=4, june_round=3) collapses to 4 for a discretionary closing entry; the broad p13 never adds on top.

June round-dollar discretionary use-it-or-lose-it, severity 3. Part of the year-end family GREATEST collapse; overlaps the round family but year-end and round are SEPARATE evidence axes (timing vs amount-shape) so they may both contribute one value each.

Manual-rail family MAX tier, severity 4. CRITICAL DEDUP: when triple-gap (accountability sev 5) fires, w_rail is set to 0 because the triple-gap IS BUILT FROM manual-rail+no-vid+no-payee — verified 8,016/8,016 triple-gap rows also fire manual-disc-rail, so summing both double-counts. Rail subsumed into the gap contribution.
Broader manual-rail net (PERSONAL SERVICES/TRAVEL etc.), severity 3. Family GREATEST(disc=4, broad=3); also zeroed when triple-gap subsumes it.
NULL/WARRANT discretionary low-weight corroborator, severity 2. Part of rail-family GREATEST. Documented as needing a co-marker before escalation; the low weight encodes that.

robust_z>=20 + ac_n>=8 discretionary peer outlier, severity 4. Pure-amount signal, runs in all years incl. thin (robust_z is amount-derived). Independent evidence axis (peer deviation) so it contributes its own value; corroborates concentration on AshBritt/Fondomonte.

Accountability family. Placeholder/no-payee on discretionary, severity 4. Family takes MAX(triple-gap=5, placeholder=4, off-contract=2). When triple-gap fires (vid NULL + manual + no payee) the placeholder is its superset, so the family value is 5 once, not 5+4.

Highest accountability tier, severity 5. Strict subset of placeholder-payee AND of manual-rail-disc. Implemented as an ESCALATOR: it sets the accountability-family value to 5 AND zeros the separately-counted manual-rail contribution (8,016 rows deduped). fiscal_year NOT IN (2019,2020) hard-coded so it never fires on the thin-year vid gap.

Named vendor + no contract_number, severity 2 (contract_number only ~2-6% populated, so absence is weak evidence). Disjoint from placeholder markers (named vs unnamed payee). Lowest tier of the accountability family GREATEST.

Off-contract discretionary procurement (moderate), severity 2. Heavily overlaps the accountability 'no contract' marker and the concentration family on the SAME vendors (UnitedHealthcare, Active Resource Mgmt). Treated as ONE off-contract evidence axis: contributes via the accountability-family GREATEST, not as an independent add, to avoid triple-counting the missing-contract fact.

NESTED tier of disc_proc_offcontract_base (off-contract + >=$1M + >=20% share). Its concentration component is already counted by sole_source_discretionary_concentration (sev 4) and its off-contract component by the accountability family. Zero independent weight — it is a tier label, not new evidence.

NESTED top tier (adds robust_z>=10). Its peer-outlier component is already counted by peer_outlier_discretionary_zextreme and its concentration/off-contract components by their families. Zero independent weight; it manifests as a high COMBINED score (AshBritt/Active Resource score 24-30) via the other families co-firing, which is the intended corroboration.

Individual-person payee in discretionary (possible related party), severity 3. Word-boundary business-keyword exclusion (no substring) per the ZTE/Aztec discipline. Independent evidence axis (payee-entity shape). 211 rows.

Masked payee on EXTERNAL rail (ACH/WARRANT) discretionary, severity 3. DEDUP: zeroed when the accountability gap family already fired >=4 on the same row (169 rows subsumed) since both encode the missing-payee fact; only its distinct external-rail-masked cases survive as independent.

Agency-level Benford elevator (5 named agencies), severity 2. Implemented as the inlined exact-agency list (recomputable, no substring). A multiplier-style elevator per spec but added as a small flat 2 points and only on non-entitlement rows; never standalone-decisive at weight 2.

model.tier_matrix:
FINAL TIER = f(magnitude band rank x risk band). Two axes, both recomputable from source fields.

AXIS 1 — Magnitude rank (from mag_band, which derives only from amount, populated every year incl. thin FY19/20):
  MB_HIGH = M1_100M+ , M2_10M-100M , M3_1M-10M   (rank<=3 ; '>= $1M' materiality)
  MB_LOW  = M4_250K-1M , M5_100K-250K , M6_25K-100K (rank>=4)

AXIS 2 — Risk band (from risk_score = marker_sum * category_risk_multiplier; bands per risk_bands field):
  R0 NONE (=0) | R1 LOW (0<s<3) | R2 MODERATE (3<=s<6) | R3 ELEVATED (6<=s<10) | R4 HIGH (s>=10)

TIER MATRIX (recompute with CASE, in this order):
  Tier 1  (top review priority)  := MB_HIGH AND (R3 OR R4)            -> high materiality AND elevated markers
  Tier 2  (secondary review)     := (MB_HIGH AND R2) OR (MB_LOW AND (R3 OR R4))  -> material+moderate, or smaller+elevated
  Tier 3  (screen/queue)         := (any remaining row with R1 OR R2 OR R3 OR R4 not caught above)  i.e. risk_score>0
  Tier 4  (high value, CLEAN)    := MB_HIGH AND R0                    -> high dollar but zero markers (e.g. the $4.66B scheduled transfer)
  Unranked (low value, clean)    := MB_LOW AND R0

VALIDATED PREVALENCE on the 306,604-row >=$100K population:
  Tier1   3,009 (0.98%)  $15.1B  — 100% discretionary (zero entitlement), surfaces AshBritt, Fondomonte, Kiewit, NaphCare-class leads
  Tier2  20,008 (6.53%)  $12.3B
  Tier3  27,368 (8.93%)  $14.4B
  Tier4  39,428 (12.86%) $291.9B — overwhelmingly entitlement mega-flows, correctly clean/no-marker (this is the 'large but benign' bucket the brief requires Tier4 to hold)
  Unranked 216,791 (70.71%) $59.4B

NOTE: Tier order = priority (1 highest). Tier 4 is deliberately 'high value but clean' per the requirement, distinct from Unranked low-value-clean. A bondholder reproduces any tier from: amount (-> mag_band), category1 (-> multiplier), and the fired markers (-> marker_sum).
model.overlap_notes:
Resolved by (a) collapsing each MARKER FAMILY to a single contribution = GREATEST tier that fires within it (families are internally nested/disjoint), and (b) explicit cross-family escalator-subsumption for the documented true double-counts. Specifics, all verified live against mart/hv_base.parquet:

1) ROUND family (round_1m / round_100k-not-1m / whole-not-10k): disjoint by construction (verified 0 overlap by the specialists). Contributes ONE value = GREATEST. A single estimate JV never scores 5+4+3.

2) DUPLICATE family: dup_diffday_discretionary (5) is a STRICT SUBSET of dup_diffday_all (2); sameday(3) is mutually exclusive with diffday. GREATEST -> a discretionary diffday scores 5, an entitlement diffday scores 2, never both.

3) ACCOUNTABILITY + MANUAL-RAIL (the biggest double-count): triple_gap (sev 5) = vid NULL + manual rail + no payee. Verified 8,016/8,016 triple-gap rows ALSO fire manual_rail_discretionary (4) AND are a subset of generic_placeholder_payee (4). The manual-rail fact and the missing-payee fact are the COMPONENTS of the triple gap, so summing them triple-counts one piece of evidence. FIX: when triple_gap fires, set the accountability family to 5 and ZERO the separately-summed manual-rail contribution (w_rail_net=0) and the placeholder external-rail entity contribution (169 rows subsumed). This dropped population avg risk_score 1.06 -> 0.87 and p99 13.5 -> 11.2.

4) OFF-CONTRACT chain: disc_proc_offcontract_base/_concentrated_large/_concentrated_peer_outlier are NESTED (each a subset of the prior) and their three components are ALREADY owned by other families — concentration by sole_source_discretionary_concentration, peer-outlier by peer_outlier_discretionary_zextreme, missing-contract by large_procurement_no_contract_reference. Only the base off-contract 'missing contract' fact is counted once (weight 2 via the accountability family GREATEST); the two nested tiers get weight 0 and instead surface as HIGH COMBINED scores when the independent families co-fire (AshBritt scores 24-30 because new-vendor + concentration + peer + off-contract are genuinely DISTINCT evidence).

5) NEW-VENDOR family: new_vendor_large (3) is a superset of new_vendor_dominant (5); short_tenure (4) overlaps both. GREATEST collapse. PLUS FY2021 contribution HALVED (floor/2, 3,893 rows) to neutralize the post-NULL-vid-gap re-appearance artifact the specialists flagged (vendors look 'new' only because FY19/20 vid was NULL).

6) YEAR-END family (p13_all/p13_disc/june_round): GREATEST. june_round overlaps the ROUND family but timing(year-end) and amount-shape(round) are SEPARATE evidence axes, so each family may contribute one value — that is corroboration, not double-count.

7) BENFORD: agency-level elevator (weight 2) deliberately co-fires with round/concentration/peer within the 5 named agencies; kept additive at low weight because it is the population-level EXPLANATION of where those cluster — combining is corroborative.

8) NON-RUNNABLE markers (appropriation_near_monopoly; off-contract nested tiers) carry weight 0 so absent mart features cannot inflate any score.

PRINCIPLE: same FACT counted once (subsume); distinct evidence axes counted additively then category-multiplied; each family capped at its own GREATEST so over-firing within a theme is impossible.
